text:
AIO to my mom wanting my managers bosses number. pretty much all of the context is there but me (f20) and my mom (f82) (i’m adopted) keep having this problem whenever i get a new job. in the past i have given her the number and she has used it to ask if i was actually at work when she didn’t believe me.

this time im going to be going to a town an hour and a half away for work because they are short staffed. yesterday my mom fell while working outside after me and the news told her to stay in because of an extreme heat warning.

i understand that she could fall again but how would my managers boss help with that. for reference i work in chain retail so she has almost nothing to do with me and has only been texting me for my hotel stay information.

so people of reddit, am i overreacting? i feel like the way i said that she didn’t need the number was too much. also her saying forget it is not her agreeing with me, its her way of saying i don’t understand what she’s saying.

*edit: obligatory thank you for the replies and supportive comments. i’ve been dealing with this behavior my entire life and a few years ago i finally decided to start standing up to it, so believe it or not this is better than is used to be lmao. i will be making absolute sure that she gets a life alert or some similar emergency device. i will not be giving her the number (never was but really wont now). working on moving out also just to ease some minds if any werent eased.*
label: NOR

text:
AIO, Should I be concerned? Will he become violent?
Throwaway account

Hi everyone.

I am 23F and been dating my bf for \~9 months. We got into a fight the other day that we still haven’t overcome. My mind is ruminating on a few things and I would love some input.

Basically he asked me to do a favor (stop by his house on the way to work to pick something up for him so I can drop it off to him)

I said yes…however I really did not want to. It was 20 mins out of my way, I was already missing 2 hours of my shift for an appointment, and on an empty gas tank. I fully acknowledged I should have told him no, and it wasn’t fair to say yes if I was going to be upset at him for it. We do work at the same place btw.

When he asked me what was wrong, I began to tell him I was very stressed (I had just paid $1500 on different bills, and was stressed about money and wanted to get clocked in.) that was all I got out before he began yelling at me.

I dropped it and went back to work.

After work we went to his house and I wasn’t talking much when finally he harshly told me that I either need to tell him what’s wrong or leave.

I told him I don’t like doing last minute favors out of my way, and BAM he’s yelling again. He said I was a man hater because I’ll run errands for other coworkers but not him. He kept yelling and finally I grabbed my bag and began to leave.

When I got to my car, he followed after me. I thought he was going to tap the window and ask me to put it down, but instead he aggressively opened the driver side of the car and lightly grabbed my forearm. It didn’t hurt.

Since then we have continued to fight off and on in between silences.

I am really concerned about the yelling, the opening my door and touching me, even if it didn’t hurt. I fear that this may escalate some day?

Am I making this a bigger deal than it is? AIO??

text:
AIO? I just found out my girlfriend of three years cheated on me, but she says it “doesn’t count” because I once jokingly gave her a hall pass.
Using a throwaway because I don’t want my girlfriend or our friends finding this.

I’ve been with my girlfriend for three years. She’s a huge fan of a popular sci-fi series, and her friends are even more obsessed with it. I’ve always supported her interests. I’ve watched a few episodes with her when she wanted me to, even though half the time I had no idea what was going on. Honestly, I just liked seeing her happy and excited about it.

About two years ago, we had her group of friends over for a games night one evening and they were all joking about which cast members they fancied. My girlfriend’s top pick was one particular actor who plays a very iconic character in the show. I even knew who the character was even though I’m not really into the show, and I’ll admit, the guy is attractive. I have eyes after all.

At some point she jokingly asked if she could have a “hall pass” for him. Since this was clearly a ridiculous hypothetical that would never happen I laughed and said yes then thought nothing more of it.

Fast forward about four months, December 2024. My gf and her friends went to a convention overseas where this actor and several others from the show were appearing. She invited me, but it was abroad and I couldn’t get the time off work. All seemed to go well for her and I was genuinely happy she had such a fun experience!

Then, a couple of weeks ago, her friend group had a falling out. One of her friends reached out to me and told me my girlfriend had used her hall pass in Germany.

At first, I honestly thought it was a sick joke. I didn’t believe it. But when I confronted my girlfriend, she admitted it without even trying to deny it.

According to her, she met the actor during an autograph session at the convention. Later that evening, she and her friends saw him at the hotel bar and approached him. They all ended up hanging out, going to a few bars together, and eventually my girlfriend and this actor were alone. They kissed. Then, later that night, she left her hotel room and went to his, where they slept together.

As I was listening to this I felt like I’d been punched in the stomach.

The worst part is that she genuinely seems to believe she didn’t do anything wrong because I had technically given her a hall pass. She admits she should have mentioned it sooner, but she’s acting like the only real mistake was not telling me immediately. She said she wants to be transparent now that it’s out, and she even showed me photos that back up the story. Which I really didn’t need to f*cking see!!

I feel sick. I hate the show now. I hate that actor. But I still love my girlfriend, and that makes me feel like an idiot.

Part of me wants to salvage this but she’s literally planning on going to vegas where he and all the other actors are going to be next month. I asked her not to but she’s spent so much money on this, she said I could come along and she’d even pay for me. She offered to introduce me to the guy to prove it’s harmless (but I think I’d just punch his stupid b list-pretty face) or I can have a hall pass from the show myself.

Am I overreacting for feeling like this was cheating, even though I jokingly agreed to the hall pass months before?
label: YOR

text:
AIO: About freeloading parents at my wedding?
My wife (40F) and I (41M) recently got married. My in-laws rented a beautiful lake house for the week that cost around $15,000, and we covered the groceries for about 15 people. Almost everyone flew in, while my parents only had a 2.5-hour drive.

My parents are retired and financially comfortable. They stayed all week, ate all the food, enjoyed the rehearsal dinner that my in-laws paid for, and my mom even let my mother-in-law pay for her spa treatment. They had a great time.

What gets me is they never offered to contribute to anything. We told everyone not to get us wedding gifts because having everyone there was enough, but most people still gave us a card or a small gift anyway.

My parents didn't even give us a card.

It's not really about the money. It's that they seemed perfectly happy to accept everyone else's generosity without making even the smallest gesture in return. A simple congratulations card would've meant more than the cash.

Am I overreacting, or would this bother you too?

tl;dr - cheap ass Boomer parents took advantage of wealthy in-laws and didn't get us a card or anything.
label: NOR

text:
AIO for not wanting to go on a family vacation?
Sorry for formatting. I'm on mobile!

For a little bit of context: I primarily live with my dad and rarely have any contact with my mom's side of the family simply because we don't talk much and I've had problems in the past with my mom and her family gossiping about me and my brother behind our backs. But that's for another story.

For years, my mom has wanted to plan a trip to Boston/Salem, MA, as a graduation gift for both me and my brother (He graduated in 2024 and I graduated this year) after out first trip in 2021. But a lot of things about the trip have been changed behind our backs. Instead of it just being me, my brother, my mom, and her husband, six other family members decided they wanted to tag along too.

I was also lied to about the dates as well, being told by my mom that the trip would be from August 18th-24th, but i was on the phone with my brother just today and was told that it will actually be August 15th-22nd. It's not that huge of a change, but I still feel like it was super important info that wasn't given accurately to me.

There have also been some fights and confusion about who gets what room in the Airbnb we're going to. When my mom told me to pick my room, I picked a rather small one with only one bed thinking I wasn't going to be rooming with anyone else, but it also turns out that my mom's husband was wanting me to room with one of my cousins because there arent enough bedrooms. Another cousin also just decided she wanted to bring her new boyfriend we didn't know about and despite the fights we've been getting into about room planning my mom told her he could come and "there's plenty of room." This is a five bedroom house with two bathrooms. A few bedrooms have multiple beds, but it's still being stretched quite thinly with who can be accommodated.

And on top of all of that I heard from my dad that when he was talking to my mom about this trip she told him that if I said a single thing about missing being home she'll put me on a bus straight back home while the others stay.

I've been stressing about this whole trip for months because I rarely talk to these people, plans are changing without anyone telling me or my brother, and I just don't want to go. I just generally hate traveling, especially with groups this big, because I have such awful anxiety about having to sleep in unfamiliar environments. The only reason I'm still thinking of going is because I don't want to disappoint my brother because he has all these little trips and outfits planned out for me, and he's wanting to take me to the Met Museum when we stay in NYC for a couple days on our way home at the end of the trip.

It's really just been a whirlwind of emotions for me about this trip, and I don't know if I'm actually overreacting about not wanting to go or not. Everyone I talk to says to just go and that it might be fun. AIO??
label: NOR

text:
AIO for not attending a family reunion I wasn't even invited to in the first place?
I saw on the family group chat last night about everyone getting excited for the family reunion this weekend. I wasn't invited.

Coincidentally, my college friends reached out to me a month ago to inform me that they all decided to gather together the same weekend in a different state. They not only invited me, but also expressed how much they'd love it if I come. I accepted that invitation no problem and am looking forward to not only hang out with great people, but also to check out a new state.

My mom called yesterday and somewhere in the conversation she mentioned the family reunion and asked if I was going. I told her that I was going to be in a different state that same weekend and since no one in the family reached out to invite me, I will not be in attendance. Her response was, "I see..." and then changed the subject.

An hour later, I get a call from one of my cousins and the call went something like this:

Cousin: "So your mom tells me you're not going to the family reunion."

Me: "That's right."

Cousin:"Why not? You don't want to see the family?"

Me: "I barely knew you guys were even having a reunion and since I wasn't invited to the reunion, I made plans to go elsewhere the same weekend."

Cousin: "You've known about this reunion for months! Why are you acting like such a drama queen!?"

Me: "First off, I only saw one mention of the reunion in March on the family group chat when grandma uploaded the backyard being remodeled and you replying, 'I CAN'T WAIT FOR THE REUNION!!! <3' on the family group chat. No one reached out to me with the date, the time, or anything about the reunion. I put two and two together that I'm not wanted at the reunion so I decided to go somewhere where I am wanted--"

Cousin: "You are SUCH a drama queen! That's the problem with you! You knew there was a reunion and you didn't even reach out!? That's your fault and then you wonder why no one reached out to you--"

Me: "There you go. You could have used this time to apologize for not informing me and then given me the information about the reunion, but instead you're hurling insults and further confirming my suspicions that I'm not wanted at the reunion--"

Cousin: "Whatever. I'm done talking to you! You're such a f***ing cry baby!"

(hangs up)

I shouldn't have let that bothered me, but I've been up all night thinking about that conversation. I think I know the answer to this one, but because my mind has been clouded with so much bs from last night, I figured I might as well ask the subreddit...

AIO?

Thank you for your time.

Edit: No, it's not AI slop. I'm legit going through this right now.
label: NOR

text:
AIO: I don’t know who to believe
My boyfriend (41M) and I (41F) have been together for about 4 years. About a week ago, I was done with work at 10PM, and when I texted him, I got no response. His location seemed to be at a bar one block from our apartment that we go to regularly, so I went there. (For clarity, we’ll call this place Block Bar.) He was not there. I screenshotted his location because it seemed strange to me. I called him repeatedly and got no answer. I kind of blew it off, and assumed something was off with the location. I fully recognize that at this point, I should’ve gone home to see if he was there but I did not. Instead I went to another bar across the street from our apartment (“Regular Bar”) and hung out with some friends.
About an hour and a half later, he texted me, said he passed out at home, and when would I be home, so I went home, which was totally fine.
The next morning I was using his computer for something and I got a weird feeling and decided to check his text messages. I discovered a text exchange that happened about 15 minutes before he called me with another regular at Regular Bar whom we are mutually friendly with.

Him: I don't know what to say. You are perfect.
Him: Can we link up tomorrow after work?
Her: Maybe ;)
Him: Thank you for an incredible evening

He used to say, "You are perfect" to me when we first started hooking up, and I know he said it to at least one other woman whom he used to hook up with. Additionally, he has a history of cheating. This woman (we'll call her Larry) also lives in the apartment building above Block Bar that showed his location when I couldn’t find him. I confronted Larry about it, and she insists that they were "on the roof smoking cigarettes," which obviously sounds absolutely absurd. For 2+ hours? And somehow while they were "smoking cigarettes" he never looked at his phone, saw that I called or texted, and completely ignored me? He says he doesn't even remember being on her roof because he was too drunk, all he remembers is waking up in our bed and trying to find me. But that timeline doesn’t make any sense. He woke up, texted her first to tell her what an “incredible evening” he had and then called me 15 minutes later?
She says they were "on the roof" for 2 hours, which, by the way, already on its own is sus because it's cold outside at night where we live, so just standing outside on a roof for hours isn't really a thing to do.
They both insist that nothing happened.
I will also say that when I talk to my boyfriend, I do believe him. Larry is not the type of woman that he normally goes for, and her whole life is already a mess on its own. Like, this whole thing is kind of like me asking him, "Did you hook up with your obnoxious, ugly cousin" and that is the only thing keeping me believing that they didn't hook up. He acknowledges how bad all of this looks, and what his conclusion would be if we were in opposite positions. But the thing is that when I objectively look at everything, I can't ignore the facts: It very much looks like he cheated.
I don't know what to do. If I choose to believe him, then I have to drop all of the reservations in my head about this because I can't say, "I believe you," and still hold it against him because that's not fair. But I can't let it go. I keep going on and on in my head about this, and how stupid I must be to not believe the evidence that's literally right in front of me, but I also very much want to believe him. I don’t know what to do or how to get over this. How should I handle this?
Halp!

Aside: Yes, I already have an appointment to get STD tested. Please refrain from giving me healthcare advice

text:
My bf controls what I wear AT HOME, AIO?
I (21F) recently moved in with my boyfriend (20M). We’ve been together for almost two years.

We live on the ground floor, have a small garden, and directly across from us is our neighbor’s garden. Our living room doesn’t have curtains yet.

At home I usually just wear normal pajamas or an oversized T-shirt without a bra. Ever since we moved in together, my boyfriend has been making constant comments about it.

For example, he’ll say:

“Be careful your T-shirt isn’t too thin, or the neighbor might see your nipples.”
If I’m wearing a long T-shirt without pants: “Be careful it doesn’t ride up, or the neighbor might see your underwear or your legs.”

Today he took it to another level.

I was just wearing a loose T-shirt and regular pants. I briefly stretched my arms, and he suddenly said, “Be careful the neighbor doesn’t look through your shirt sleeve and see your breasts.”

I actually had to ask him to repeat himself several times because I thought I’d misheard him. Who intentionally looks into someone’s T-shirt sleeve from that far away?

At that point I got really angry and told him to stop constantly policing what I wear at home. I’m in my own apartment, and I don’t think it’s my responsibility to live as if the neighbor is watching every movement with a telescope.

Out of frustration I said, “If you keep bothering me about this, I’ll go outside into the garden naked.”

I wanted him to promise that he would stop controlling what I wear at home, but he refused.

Out of spite, I took off my pants but kept my underwear on, then lifted my T-shirt for a few seconds to show him how ridiculous I thought his logic was.

He immediately took a picture of me and sent it to my mother.

I called my mom right away and explained what had happened. She told me that his behavior was completely abnormal.

When he realized that sending it to my mother hadn’t intimidated me, he said, “You’re cheap. I’ll just send it to my friends then. If you don’t care that the neighbor might see you in your underwear, then you shouldn’t care if other people see you in your underwear either.”

After that we argued a lot and I tried to make him understand how wrong his words and actions were, and when he refused to understand I said I'm breaking up with you.
He then started having a panic attack and threw up and said he can't without me, and asked me to take him back. He apologised a lot.

He said all the hard work he does is for me, which is true he does work very hard everyday and has done a lot for me and shown me his love and patience many times.

A day passed and he kept on apologising throughout the day saying how sorry he was.

I forgave him but it still kinda weighs on my heart.

text:
AIO about my girlfriends social media
I (23m) have been with my girlfriend (25f) for about 5 years . She has a bubbly personality and is very attractive. I am not the most articulated person and have suppressed my feelings for a long time but I’ve been trying to better understand what causes these emotions. In arguments I usually roll over because it’s easier and I usually feel like I’m in the wrong, it’s hard to build an opinion on feelings I don’t fully understand, she is much much better at these things than I.

I saw someone had messaged her on her Instagram while we were looking at reels on her phone. I asked her who that was out of curiosity, and she replied, “I don’t know.” I asked her to open it. She refused and instead said, “We can open it in a few minutes or in the morning; I don’t want to encourage him.” The longer I thought about it, the more it ate at me. I’m not typically jealous, and I don’t go through her phone; I don’t know if it’s because I trust her or if I’d actually find something and refuse to do it. I asked if we could again, and this time she said, “We can in the morning.” This triggered something for me, and I couldn’t help but think, why? She continued scrolling. I pushed again a little bit later, and she repeated the same thing, but I couldn’t stop thinking about it. She opened his profile, and we went through it; she had no idea who he was, but she knew he’d been following her for forever. Finally, I got her to open it. I know she would’ve done the same thing if the roles were reversed. But through the years, with all her stories, he had made comments about her and her body, and this was that. He had said things like, “That hourglass ❤️.” Once, she replied, “You’re too sweet to me.” I wasn’t upset by this; I knew this kind of thing happened. She got mad at me for having her open it, and I explained I was just curious and knew she got that kind of attention often.

She seemed flustered and just scrolled through her stories. She has lots of videos and pictures with her friends—traveling, dancing, and all the outfits she likes. I noticed I wasn’t in any of them. It didn’t matter to me much, except that I knew I had been on there before, but she had removed me from everything except one collage where I was a speck in the corner with her dog, barely noticeable among all the content.

I asked if that was the only one of me left, and she said no—that I was in another post—and pointed at it without opening it. She scrolled to the bottom of her page and told me which picture I was in without actually opening it. I could tell she knew I wasn’t in there, so I said, “I want to see; I like that picture.” Before she even opened it, she said, “Well, I don’t know if I deleted it or not because I was mad at you.” Lo and behold, I was not in it.

This didn’t bother me; I assumed she just said it to try to make me feel more comfortable. But she instantly got more upset with me for making her open it. It felt like she was trying to defend herself continuously, even though I didn’t say she did anything wrong, and she interrupted anything I tried to say. I wasn’t upset about anything except the fact that she kept getting angrier with me, saying I didn’t trust her and that I had called her an “attention wh**e.”

I guess I’m more confused why she reacted the way she did, I even thought she would be more apologetic about her responding to that person or show any sign of sympathy for me instead of attacking me for the things I am doing.
label: NOR

text:
AIO for crying after my boyfriend answered our friends question
Me and my boyfriend were at a fastfood restaurant with one of our friends , our friend asked us both :

" if you call have a free pass without any problems afterwards who would it be , it has to be a celebrity"

I didn't have an answer and I still don't because I don't think I'd want anyone else. He didn't have an answer back then either. This was about two weeks ago . After we left I forgot about the question and I never really thought about it anymore.

Now today he randomly out off the blue said: " I have an answer to friends question"

I told him that was a random thing to say an I don't know if I even wanted to know since I've been very insecure after giving birth last year.

Ofcourse I still asked who because I'm dumb and I wanted to know even tho I knew it would hurt. He told me who it was and it's a blonde hardcore dj who looks pretty basic (very pretty nothing wrong with basic) however I'm alternative with coloured hair , tattoos and piercings.

My brother was here too and ask to see because he didn't know her . He just shrugged and said "kinda basic". At this point I already felt a little hurt even tho I asked who because I was curious who'd be worth it to him. And then he said to my brother :

" What's wrong with basic , sometimes I love basic".

That comment shattered my heart and I went into the garden for a cigarette and a cry.

I feel so empty but yet so sad and hurt and I'm here in the garden wondering if I'm making a big deal out of nothing since I asked who. On the other side our friend asked that question so long ago and he wasn't even here when he told me he knew his answer and I never even though about it ever again. Maybe I'm overreacting because I did start my period and I'm in so much pain I might be reflecting that in my reaction but I just don't know anymore.

We didn't talk about it yet because I'm scared I'm making a big deal out of nothing but I can see he feels bad but also irritated...

text:
AIO about what happened?
So at the beginning of the semester, I burned out working my ass off for high marks for the last semester's exams. Going into this semester, I was drained and dealing with a lot of fights at home. I slacked off a bit on assignments early on, but I knew I’d get back up.

My science teacher immediately started treating me like a troublemaker. She would make subtle, passive aggressive remarks to indirectly call me stupid. During labs, she looked at me like I was dumb for being confused by her poorly worded worksheets, while even my teammates were totally confused. She once walked over, sighed, and said, "Why do you always not do what the instructions say?" like we were too stupid to read. (Mind you that she was known to other students as a mean and terrible teacher)

Then I had detention because I was late 3 times, which was my fault, and I took responsibility for it. But she never gave me a warning like she did with other students. During the 15 minute lunch detention, she loudly announced to older students hanging out in the room that me and another girl were in detention, and told them to "watch us" while she left. She sounded said it in a way that humiliated me and I knew she could tell. I had froze in shock as my mind went into freeze mode as I have never been treated so badly by a teacher before.

The stress from burnout, home issues, and her treatment made my hair to fall out when I ran my hands through it. I got dark circles, lost sleep, and I remember looking in the mirror feeling like I was looking at a total stranger. I was so mentally drained I started failing all my classes, which was something I’ve never done before.

My mom emailed her about my struggles and how her comments hurt me. A week later, during a chemistry unit I actually studied hard for and understood and felt more confident in my work than the past few weeks, but then I was called to guidance. And after a long talk about how I was feeling and talking about my plans. We decided I should switch to a different science class.

When I went to get the teacher to sign the transfer paper, and I thought it would be all over, she gave one last subtle remark as a last jab. She gave me that same look she gave before like she was looking down on me, like I was too stupid for her class and said, "This is probably a wise decision, isn't it?" I froze again and couldn't defend myself as my mind went to freeze mode again.

After leaving, I started gaslighting myself, thinking maybe I was just lazy and the problem. But the moment I left her class, my grades in all my classes shot up significantly. My highest mark ended up being in science. And then thought that I most likely aren't the problem as I was just entirely drained by my home life and her toxic environment.

Edit: Thank you for everyone who has been so sweet and empathetic, I really feel supported by all your sweet comments and suggestions! I'm really glad I had shared my story on here as I was really debating on deleting it as I had tried posting it on another subreddit and I got really hurt by one specific comment that had said "YOR. stop making excuses out of minor struggles" which had made me second guess myself about whether I should or shouldn't post it for others opinions but the comments I got here really made me feel a lot better so I thank ALL of you for your support and kindness ❤🙃🥰
label: NOR

text:
AIO for being startled by my gf?
So my gf (we are both women!) and I have been together for years. Approaching 30, cute apartment, really building a life together. She’s smart, loyal, and reliable, a wonderful partner in so many ways. In recent months I’ve noticed she’s gotten increasingly more and more easily agitated during conflict, like quicker to raise her voice and more sensitive. She mentioned a couple weeks ago that she feels like when she makes suggestions I don’t really listen or take it into account, I can admit I can be somewhat “my way or the highway” but it’s because I research every single thing and have a lot of research to back everything I do but she’s more of a gut feeling gal. She said when I don’t listen to her opinion it makes her feel unheard which in turn makes her feel like raising her voice is the only way to get a point across.

The other day we were talking about the best approach to get our cat to start eating wet food. My gf feeds her in the morning, so I asked if she could forgo the usual dry food and instead give her the new wet food. She said she’d rather not because it’s a lot easier to give her the dry food in the morning when she’s rushing out the door (I work from home so I give her lunch, I get up at 8am. She works out of the home and gets up at 5am). I told her that annoyed me because it would only take a few extra minutes. She got frustrated and went to the bedroom. I went in there with her and she said she felt like yet again I was not taking something she said into consideration, that waking up that early already sucks and she’d rather just stick to the dry food and I give her the wet food at lunch. I said she should be willing to do a small extra thing since I scoop the litter more often anyway.

We went back and forth for a bit, turned off the lights, and she went under the covers facing away from me. I was sitting up in bed next to her and kept asking her why she was making a big deal out of this and we shouldn’t argue over something so small. She just gave one word answers. I kept saying it’s just wet food. Then randomly she shoots up from under the covers and is in a sitting up position and goes “why can’t you just let it go!”

I wasn’t expecting her to sit up that quickly! It startled me. For context I have a deep fear of being harmed in relationships due to relationships I have seen around me and I regularly read about harmful relationships and stuff like that so when she shot up like that, sitting up from a sleeping position and it was dark I was startled. I was like “wth are you trying to scare me!!” And she was so confused and went “what? No. Why would you ask that? Like I’m just such a scary person or something”. Then she went to the couch in the living room. I felt bad so i asked her to just come back to bed and we can talk the next day. She came to bed, and cried a lot because i think she was just overwhelmed. 🙁

I know she was not trying to startle me but I cant help my reactions to things. I get a panic response when in conflict of any kind and sudden movements scare me. We did talk about the whole thing and had a good resolution but idk if I was reasonable or not.
label: YOR

text:
AIO for privately telling my friend her TikTok could be interpreted as racially insensitive? I (28F, Black) need some outside opinions because my friend (27F, white) and I seem to have reached a dead end with this conversation, and I want to know if other people would’ve interpreted this the way I did.

My friend posted a TikTok that said:
“POV: I’m very white and don’t listen to rap idk what to do.”

One commenter basically said, “Just listen and sit there? What do you have to do girl?” and she replied, “Not relate to anything maybe? Idk???” She also posted a TikTok story saying that people being upset over her not liking rap was “comical.”

I texted her privately because, to me, the way she framed the TikTok made it seem like something much bigger than just not liking rap music. Saying she was “very white,” didn’t know what to do, and then saying she didn’t relate to rap made it feel like she was treating rap like it was weirdly foreign to her because she was white. It also felt like it reduced an entire genre to one stereotyped type of experience rather than recognizing how broad the genre actually is.

I specifically told her I didn’t think she meant anything malicious. I was trying to explain how I thought strangers would interpret it and why I thought it had the potential to blow up for the wrong reasons.

She responded saying I was reading way too deep into it and that I was “part of the problem.”

Later, she explained that she was actually talking about one specific song that was playing in the car and that the lyrics were why she doesn’t listen to rap. My response was that if that was her point, that’s not what the TikTok communicated. To me, both the TikTok and her comment made it sound like she was talking about rap as a whole, not one specific song.

She also told me she’d talked to a Black man she was staying with and that he agreed everyone was just being too sensitive. I sarcastically replied, “Well as long as he thinks you’re good then it’s fine,” because it felt like she was using that as the end of the conversation instead of engaging with what I was actually trying to explain.

She did end up deleting the TikTok after I texted her, but she still says she did absolutely nothing wrong.

So I’m curious:
Was I reading way too much into the TikTok?
Did the TikTok communicate something different than what she says she intended?
Was I out of line for privately warning her before strangers got ahold of it?

I’m including screenshots of the TikTok comments and our text conversation (with identifying information covered) because I’d rather people judge based on what was actually said than just my summary.
label: YOR

text:
AIO? Got a wedding invite mailed to us, but it was only for my bf and not me.
My (28F) boyfriend (29M) has this good friend from high school, let’s call her Sarah (26F). They were part of a trio friend group and he looks at her a bit like a little sister. My bf and I have been together for 4 years, and we have all hung out with Sarah and a group of friends multiple times over the years. Sarah is not always at these gatherings (hosted by a mutual couple-friend) but when she is, she’s always been nice to me, we’ve had plenty of friendly one-on-one conversations and I even got her for Secret Santa one year. She definitely does give off “little sister” energy when she’s interacting with my bf.

Anyways…about 6 months ago, we received an invitation in the mail to Sarah’s wedding reception. She and her fiancée had a courthouse wedding in the summer with no attendees, so this was the event that they planned to celebrate with friends and family. My bf RSVP’d for us simply by texting her and letting her know we were coming, which I guess she acknowledged but I don’t know exactly what she said over text, which is context for what’s next.

Fast forward to last weekend: I realized while talking to my bf about holiday plans that Sarah’s wedding dinner is in 2 weeks, and I remind him that we need to get her a gift and ask if she has a registry, plus I ask if he knows the dress code since the invitation didn’t specify one (typical man to not know any of the details of a wedding lol). He said he’d reach out and ask those questions and let me know.

A few days ago I followed up to see if he had talked to Sarah yet, and I noticed he got immediately tense/uncomfortable. He just sighed and tells me that we aren’t going anymore. Shocked, I asked why/what happened and he tells me that when he reached out and asked those questions, Sarah told him that only HE was invited to the dinner (not me). He told me that she said that it was supposed to be “really intimate” with only the closest friends of hers attending and that “no one is getting a plus one, so it’s not just you [my bf].”

My bf is really a huge advocate for me so he apparently told her that he wasn’t coming without me and that the invitation was misleading, she said she wouldn’t budge on this, and that was that. Apparently, now they have unfollowed each other on all socials and the other friend-group that hosts all the hangouts is texting him asking him to mend things up and still come to the dinner because it would mean a lot to her.

Personally, I’m conflicted on my feelings. On the one hand, I am upset/offended that I got personally “disinvited” to the dinner only a few weeks before. FWIW, there is going to be roughly 35 people (per Sarah) at this dinner which is at a local restaurant in our city, and the guests are ordering off menu so no per-plate fee or anything. Also, the restaurant is owned by her fiancée so it actually probably costs nothing out of pocket but I don’t know that for sure. I’m more offended by the fact that I’m looked at as “my bf’s plus-one” when he have been together for years and own the house together that she sent the invite to.

On the other hand, I understand it’s her day and she’s allowed to invite or not invite anyone she chooses. I can somewhat understand her anger towards my bf trying to force her to invite me after she had already decided not to, but she’s always struck me as a little bit spoiled growing up and not used to not getting everything she wants in a situation. But now I feel bad (guilty?) that my bf is going to lose this 10+ year friendship over this, and the other friends are pressuring him/guilting him into coming without me which he won’t do.

So, am I (we) overreacting to this? I don’t know how to feel. Thanks in advance.
label: YOR

text:
AIO
So I'll just start from the beginning.

My partner (23M) and I (24F) were getting ready for church. I put on a cute long dress (church appropriate, of course) and a little knitted sweater over top to cover my shoulders. He looked at me and said, "What are you wearing? You look like a Mennonite." 🫠

Whatever, it didn't really bother me. He tends to pick at me any chance he gets.

What did bother me was what happened on the way to church. We were walking there from our car, and he was about 5 feet ahead of me the entire time. He's done it before, but I've never said anything. This time I calmly asked, "Why do you always walk ahead of me? I find it a bit rude and disrespectful." He didn't respond and just kept walking.

An hour later, after church, we're walking back to the car and once again he's several feet ahead of me. For context, I'm 4'11" and he's 6'3". I was also wearing heels, so I was taking my sweet time. I understand our walking speeds are different, but I asked him again why he was walking ahead of me.

He responded in an angry tone: "Well, I can't walk slower, and there are cars coming. You can't be walking that slow on the street."

I honestly didn't feel like I was asking for much. What bothered me more was how aggressively he responded. We drove home in silence.

When we got home, he said, "I don't understand why you have to make such a big deal out of this. It's not that serious."

I replied, "It's a big deal to me, and it's serious to me. Why can't you just accept that?"

He said, "Well, I don't care about that. It's not important to me. Be a woman. Stop being a child."

For context, he often uses "you're being a child" during disagreements.

At that point I got upset and said, "How are you telling me to be a woman when you're not even half a man?"

He replied, "I am a man," and then left for work.

Since Sunday, he's been sleeping in the spare room. Today is Wednesday, and we've mostly been ignoring each other.

Yesterday, one of our sheep had babies. He asked if I wanted to go see them, and I said sure. When we got back home, I asked where my apology was. He responded, "Well, where's my apology?"

I asked, "What am I apologizing for?"

He said, "For saying I'm not a man."

I just got up and went to my room.

Like am I in the wrong expecting an apology for how he spoke to me and dismissed my feelings before apologizing for my comment, or am I looking at this the wrong way?

text:
AIO about my husband’s social life after we had a baby?
My husband and I have a 4-month-old baby, and I genuinely can’t tell anymore whether my expectations are unreasonable or whether we’re just seeing parenthood and marriage completely differently.

Since our daughter was born, my husband has gone out with his friends (“boys only”) once or twice a month. At first it was not a big deal at all, but he seems to want to go out more often and these first months were incredibly hard, so I honestly expected that having a baby would naturally shift our priorities toward spending more time together as a family.

To be clear, he’s supportive if *I* want to go out with friends. The problem is… I don’t really want to. I work full-time now, so during the week I only get about two hours a day with my daughter before she goes to bed. Naturally, all I want on weekends is to spend time with her and with us as a family.

Now that summer is here, here’s what’s planned:

- A 4-day boys-only trip.
- The following weekend, a boys’ bar night with two friends.
- The weekend after that, another full Sunday with the boys because friends are visiting from out of town.
Plus a few additional evenings out.

When I finally said, “Enough is enough,” he told me I ruin the mood, that I’m unreasonable, and that I always make myself into a martyr and cry.

One thing that really bothers me is that when I come home from work, he’ll say something like, “Want to watch cartoons?” My reaction is always, “No. I only have two hours with our baby before bedtime.” He also just got home from work, so it honestly shocks me that he doesn’t seem to have the same instinct to spend those precious hours with our daughter or with us as a family. Instead he’d happily watch TV or play computer games.

I worry that he fundamentally doesn’t understand how much life changes after becoming a parent. He says he *has* changed his lifestyle, but I honestly don’t see it.

On top of that, we recently bought a house, and there is so much to do. But almost everything falls on me. If I ask him to do something, he resists, says I’m controlling, or tells me that things are fine and it’s only my problem because I care about them.

And I’m not talking about perfection or cosmetic projects. I mean basic things like:

- cleaning the toilet,
- cleaning the gutters,
- fixing a door that won’t close.

I don’t even bring up mowing the lawn or maintaining the outside because that feels completely unrealistic at this point.

Our daily lives also look very different.

I wake up at 4 a.m. to feed the baby, leave early so I’m at work by 7, leave work at 4, get home around 6, spend time feeding and playing with our daughter, put her to bed, shower, and go to sleep around 9 just so I can function the next day.

He works much closer to home. He comes home, plays computer games, kisses the baby, gives her a bottle, and then after I go to bed, goes back to gaming.

I don’t know if this is important at all, but he is 6 years older and makes roughly 20% more money than me, but I would say we are both decent earners.

To be fair, he does change diapers on weekends about as much as I do. He bottle-feeds her. He’s not an absent father in the sense that he refuses all childcare.

But I carry literally all of the invisible work:

- ordering diapers, wipes, clothes, and supplies,
- tracking what we need,
- scheduling every pediatrician appointment,
- remembering everything,
- planning everything.

He does essentially none of that. Honestly, sometimes his presence creates more work because I have to remind him about everything.

So from his perspective, he’s a great husband and father, and I’m impossible to please.

From my perspective, I feel completely alone, unloved, and like I’m begging someone to want to spend time with their own family.

Another issue that really hurt me:

One of his close friends is married to a woman who recently told me she doesn’t want to be friends with me anymore because me having a baby “highlights how different our lifestyles are.”

That really hurt. My husband says he’ll continue being friends with her husband because their friendship is separate. Part of me feels like he should stand up for me and create some distance after someone treated his wife that way.

So I honestly want to know:

⁠How often is it normal for a husband/father of a 4-month-old to have boys-only trips or full days with friends?

⁠Is it unreasonable that I’d rather we spend weekends together as a family, especially now that I only get two hours a day with my daughter during the week?

⁠My view is that occasional boys’ nights (maybe once a month, ideally on a weekday) are totally fine, one boys’ trip during the summer is also fine, but repeated weekends away or full Sundays with friends should be family time. Friends are always welcome at our house, and I’d much rather spend time with other couples or families together.

⁠Am I unreasonable for feeling hurt that my husband wants to stay close friends with someone whose wife explicitly told me she doesn’t want to be friends with me anymore because I became a mother?

I’m genuinely asking because at this point I’m questioning my own reality. He thinks I’m controlling and impossible to satisfy. I feel like I’m asking for what should be a pretty normal vision of family life. I honestly don’t know anymore who’s seeing this more clearly.
label: NOR

text:
AIO - Boyfriend Engagement Ring Shopping
**UPDATE** I had a convo with my partner, and I shared with him a lot of the great info you all provided (that a lab grown diamond is a real
Diamond, and all the info in regard to going to a local jeweler) he informed me that he has a plan, and it does not involve his moms ring entirely - she however, had hoped he would use that ring, and that is why his mom/brother brought up using that ring specifically (he mentioned it was already welded to a wedding band and a 1 year anniversary band, so it wouldn’t work anyway in his opinion) he told me he had brought up to his mom the idea of using the stone from her ring into a setting that was more “me” also, he reassured me that while he may use the $500 ring from Etsy as inspiration, he did take into account that those rings would not be quality for every day use, so he is on top of getting a ring that would suit my style, and withstand “every day wear” (although, we both agreed it would be better to wear silicone or cheaper rings for everyday use, due to our lifestyles and my tendency to loose things). In the end - it all worked out great and we had a peaceful and reassuring conversation about the whole thing. 😁😁😁 thanks everyone for the kind messages and the help!!!! **END OF UPDATE**

Just to preface I am 21F and my boyfriend is 22M, we have lived together for a year. and been together for 3 years this summer.

From recent whisperings between boyfriends parents, and his brother, I have recently discovered I am getting engaged soon!!! (YAY!)

My partner spoke to me about 3 months ago about rings and we went to try some on, during this time he expressed that his mom has a ring from her previous marriage (the one prior to her marriage to my boyfriends dad). As far as I know, it is a round diamond with a simple gold band. I think what my partner was hinting at was for him to use the ring, just resized.... (He expressed that it is a real diamond, not lab grown, so it would be worth a lot more than anything he could afford)

The ring I have showed him that I would like is a $500 ring off of Etsy, it's a teardrop/pear ring with a simple gold band. (I'm asking for something on the lower end of price for rings since I ALWAYS loose rings, so I would hate for him to pay thousands on a ring just for me to loose it in the first year)

The ring has come up a few times, and every time he brings up his mom's ring I tell him it would mean a lot to me if he proposed with a ring that fit my style, in addition, I am quite a superstitious person - and being proposed to with a ring/diamond that came out of a "failed" marriage just doesn't sit right with me.... I feel like it's a bad memento. But my partner has reassured me that no ring is going to make our marriage fail as it won't change anything about the people we are.

As we get closer to when he may propose, I grow fearful that he will propose with his mom's ring, and my reaction when he opens that ring box will be something that sours the moment. Either way - I am going to be overjoyed by the proposal, the act of him getting on one knee speaks mountains more than any ring he could have in that box. However, he has gotten bad haircuts before - and as hard as I try to smile and tell him he is still as handsome as ever - he can see straight through me. Most of the time he can tell my emotion from miles away. He has a gift.

Am I overreacting? It's just a ring. But then at the same time, it is the ring that will be with me for the rest of my life, something that represents the love and devotion I have to him, so I don't want that symbol to be something I "settled" for. And I really don't want to just buy a new ring. To me, there is something symbolic about the ring he picks out, that he orders, and that he has planned. But I think his mom is just trying to save him a few bucks.....

Also - financially, my partner is in a good financial setting. He has a stable job, we split all of the home bills (and have access to each others personal bank accounts), he goes out to eat all the time, pays for video games, goes out to do activities with his friends, and buy new hobby materials and things to fulfill his materialistic need. So for a $500 ring for his future wife shouldn't be a immense amount of pain.... right?!?!....

One last thing - if this ring was a family heirloom, that would make the worlds entirely different for me, I am joining a family and continuing a lineage, so of course I would gladly boast that ring if it was a family heirloom. But it is not. It is a ring that was tossed in the back of a jewelry box after a divorce and not thought of again until my boyfriend told his parents he was going to propose....

Am I being greedy?.... I feel greedy when I talk about this so I try not to tell him how I feel about his moms ring (other than the first time he brought it up)

text:
AIO for not accepting my girlfriend's reassurance?
Me (23m) and my girlfriend (23f) revisited a situation that happened a few days ago and I can't get out of my head about it.

We have been together for over a year, but this situation happened three months into dating. We were in an airbnb exploring a new city together and wanted to experience the city's nightlife. For context, she used to be a party girl before we got together and is no stranger to alc*h*l, but I was completely straight edge for my entire life up to that point. I still have little to no experience alcohol or dealing with int*xicated people. The majority of my exposure has been my friends drinking socially and getting a buzz, but never drunk. Well, that night she asked me to buy her drinks (she always gets drinks when we go out) and she ended up getting 4 vodka cranberries. I thought very little of it because it was over the course of 3 hours and I trusted her to know her limit. Plus, 4 drinks didn't sound like an outright scary amount, she was casually sipping on them throughout the night, and she seemed to have her wits about her still. However, I believe the issue arose when she chugged the last one because the club was closing soon.

We went to catch our uber to get back to the airbnb and that's when I noticed she was walking a bit funny. She wasn't falling over herself, but she was struggling with walking in a straight line. She would slowly start drifting off at an angle, and did this a few times before holding onto my arm for the rest of the walk. I asked her how dr*nk she was, one of a handful of times that night, and like every other time she said she's only a little tipsy. I didn't know how to gauge that exactly because again, I have never been dr*nk before so I only had her word to go on. I pushed her about it jokingly a few more times, mentioning how she had to hold onto my arm, but she assured me she was fine. Her speech wasn't slurred and everything else seemed pretty normal, besides her speaking a bit less than usual and possibly her eyes squinting more.

When we got back to the airbnb, I helped her get ready for bed. She walked me through how to do her nighttime routine. I took her makeup off, showered her, braided and tied up her hair, and put her PJs on. It's not an uncommon thing for her to ask for princess treatment like that, so I just thought of it as her wanted to be pampered. Looking back, it may have been a sign that I ignored.

We both got in bed, kissed goodnight, and I closed my eyes to go to sleep. However, my girlfriend started indicating she wanted intimate time. I shut down the idea initially because she had been drinking, but she insisted and kept getting more touchy. After a few minutes, I stopped her and asked her if she was certain and checked again about how dr*nk she felt. She said she felt fine and that she was completely certain about doing more. For reference, we had been intimate many times prior, but alcohol was never in the equation before, especially because I was always concerned about the ability to consent. After her reassurance, I made the decision to let things progress and we did have intimate time.

The next day is when I first realized that she may not have been entirely truthful the night before. I mentioned how much I appreciated her teaching me how to take care of her and enjoyed doing so. Her response seemed like she was trying to roll with what I was saying, but it kind of lacked a confidence that alerted me she may not remember the events of last night. That's when alarms started sounding in my head mentally and I started asking her specific questions. She tried playing it off and guessing answers, but that's when I found out that she apparently blacked out. I started feeling sick to my stomach. That was my concern the whole night before and it felt like my fears came true. She only remembered dancing at the club at the beginning and a small portion of our intimate time together. Although she kept a very positive attitude and outlook regarding the night, which I also did prior, I felt like my feelings were flipped completely around.

I kept feeling this way for weeks, but slowly came to a more neutral territory regarding it, especially after remembering all the memes she would send me before and after the incident essentially about women wanting to be intimate after having a few drinks. I eventually stopped bring it up because all she could do was try to reassure me while I couldn't accept the reassurance. It has been at the back of my mind for a while, until this past weekend when I saw a video online where people were discussing whether or not its okay to be intimate with a partner if one of them had been drinking. I brought a similar question up to my girlfriend, which was "if I were as dr*nk as you were that night, would you be intimate with me?" She said most likely not because she is "much more persuasive" and "would convince me to go to sleep." When she said those words, my heart sank. In my mind, that reads as if I have little self-control. I acknowledge I was seeking some NORating for my decision to give in that night, but hearing she wouldn't do the same and her reasoning felt like a gut punch. She tried to reassure me by saying that everything was okay and that she doesn't regret anything, but it still doesn't feel right.

Ever since she said this I can't get my mind off of it. I feel horrible and like I selfishly betrayed my own sense of morality. I had started drinking a bit a few months ago with her, but I have yet to feel anything more than just a buzz. Now though, I'm completely rethinking all of that. More reasonably, I feel like I don't want her drinking around me anymore. On top of my bad decisions, I realize I can't trust her word when she's dr*nk because I don't know what is and isn't the truth and she is functional while very int*xicated. More unreasonably, I am considering if I should even continue drinking at all. I know that me drinking has no direct correlation with the story, but now I have a bad taste in my mouth regarding alc*h*l.

AIO? Any and all advice is welcome because I feel like this is eating away at me

EDIT: To be clear, she used to be a party girl. She doesn't drink that much anymore, mostly socially, and that was the only time she went past her limit while with me.

text:
AIO for arguing with my friend after she called me “fatspo” and disgusting?
I'll start by saying that my best friend (F19) and I (F19) have both struggled with eating disorders for about three years. I feel like I’m close to recovery now. I live a relatively “normal” life and try to support my best friend as much as I can.

My BMI fluctuates between 20 and 22, I’m at a normal weight for my height, I exercise a lot, and I’m trying to improve my diet. These details will be important later.

About a week ago,we were at the school yard eating something we had either got from the cafeteria or brought from home, and somehow the conversation turned to food. Honestly, I don’t remember exactly how the chat developed, but it ended with my friend openly telling me that sometimes she looks at me and just my appearance alone makes her lose her appetite. She called me “fatspo” (for normal people: it’s a mix of the words fat and inspiration) Basically, I’m a living example of what she thinks will happen to her if she eats too much.

And she finds that disgusting. To be honest, I nearly fainted when I heard that. In my opinion, it’s not just a fucked up thing to say about another person, what makes it even worse is that I’ve suffered from the same disorder she has, and I know perfectly well how deeply upsetting and triggering those kinds of comparisons can be for someone with an eating disorder.

It hurt me deeply, both as a healthy person and as someone who has been sick too.

What’s ironic is that she used to criticize people who post photos from social media of others they think look “disgusting” and she would say that doing that was vile. But when I brought that up, she told me that using someone you know in real life as fatspo is actually much more ethical than reposting photos from strangers’ social media pages.

I blocked her everywhere and I don’t want to see her. She came to my home several times and said she had only told me the truth and that I shouldn’t be offended because she has a mental illness or something like that. A couple of mutual friends also told me I should forgive her because of her disorder.

But honestly, her comment hurt me too deeply. I don’t know whether I should forgive her and ee should keep being friends. I really wish she would recover and that we could go back to being emotionally close as before all of this.

I just don’t know what to do...
label: NOR

text:
AIO, my “friend” cut a chunk from my hair and I want to pursue legal action.
Hello Reddit,

This is a throwaway account. I (22F) have been friends with Emma (21F, fake name) for almost four years. We met in college during orientation and one of the first things she said to me is that she loves how long and beautiful my hair is. For context, my grandfather is Oneida (a First People’s Tribe in Wisconsin) and has instilled important cultural beliefs in my parents, siblings and me. In our culture, hair is only cut in times of grieving. Thankfully, none of my close family have passed in about 11 years, so my hair reaches my waist.

Nowadays, trimming about an inch of hair for maintenance of split ends is common, but large chops are very sacred and reserved for very significant periods of mourning. When I told Emma I needed a hair trim for split ends, she told me that she often trims the hair of her siblings and offered to trim mine for free. I would usually have my mother cut my hair but I am out of state for college, so that wasn’t an option.

When she came over, I explained in detail to Emma the cultural significance of cutting my hair, and she agreed to cut as little hair as possible, only getting the split ends. She agreed, and began the haircut. She pulled all my hair behind my back so I could not see how high she was cutting, but at the very first cut I felt a lot of weight leave my head. I immediately jumped up and turned to the mirror, seeing that she had chopped about 1/3 of my hair to the bottom of my shoulder blade. It must have been at least 10 inches of hair.

I screamed bloody murder. I yelled, asking why the hell she did that, and she said was cutting it at the highest split ends she saw. I know that my split ends only reach about an inch from my ends since I have no layering in my hair, so either she is confused or a total liar.

I ran out of the bathroom where she was cutting my hair into my bedroom, slamming the door behind me. She knocked at the door trying to get me to come out for about 15 minutes, and then left. I cannot describe how irate I am. I feel so betrayed, I am sick to my stomach. This happened only an hour ago, and I am still crying. She knows the significance of this; I see my hair as an extension of my identity.

I can’t wrap my head around the fact that she might have done this on accident, I truly don’t think she could be that stupid. My fear is that she has some kind of jealousy over my hair as she often compliments it, and wanted to ruin it out of jealousy. In this moment, I am seriously considering pressing legal charges if possible, but I should cool off a bit before making any big decisions. If I reported her to police, would I be overreacting?

UPDATE / MORE INFO: I have cooled off somewhat and done a bit of research on the legal side of this, and it’s true that it likely has no legal merit. As some of you explained in comments, this is not a legal issue, so thank you to those who explained it kindly. In my anger, I jumped to the extreme, but it’s true that it would be more pain than it’s worth. Now I am viewing this as a friendship/personal matter, and I doubt that I could forgive Emma for this.

It’s true, Emma is not a professional hair stylist, as some of you pointed out. Yes, in hindsight it was stupid of me to trust a friend instead of a professional, but I could not have predicted that she would do this. She has trimmed her siblings hair and two of our mutual friends’ hair and has never done something like this.

There was a verbal agreement between her and I where I held up my fingers showing about an inch and told her to cut that much off.

She has called me a few times since she left but I have not picked up, as I would likely regret what I would say because I am so upset right now. For now, I have collected the cut hair from the bathroom and am storing it in a bag until I go home for summer break. When I am home, I will burn the hair with my family, as is tradition to release the stored energy from the hair.

I think I will speak to Emma tomorrow, and I will explain the situation to our mutual friends and my family, so I will update the post tomorrow.
label: NOR

text:
AIO - No contact with my mother after she called my 4 month old a “pervert”.
Help. Here’s the story, you can read for more context afterwards.

My boyfriend (22M) and I (22F) both work, but I currently only work 3 days out of the week. For those 3 days, we mostly have my parents (57F, and 65M) babysit our 4 month old baby. His parents (unsure of mom’s age, dad is 60M) are usually busy and will babysit occasionally.

Today, I left work early. I arrived at my parents house to pick up the baby, and things seemed very normal.

I pointed out to my mother that my baby’s diaper seemed very full. She replied, “I just changed him! He pees so much!” Which didn’t concern me, because he really does. Then afterwards, she added “and not to mention, he gets a boner every time I change him!”

This caught me completely off guard and I was shocked. She then said, “He’s just a pervert. All boys are perverts! For their entire lives!”.

I was so repulsed by what she had just said. Before I could even say anything, my sisters husband - who is visiting us from out of state with my sister - said “yeah! Just wait till he’s about 2 or 3. He’ll start tugging on it. It’ll be his new tug toy.”

After this I asked for them to stop talking. I felt so repulsed and uncomfortable, but they didn’t stop.

My mom continued and said, “oh yeah, and when he’s a teenager, he’ll he doing it all the time.”

At this point, I was seriously yelling at them to stop, and that it was wrong and making me very uncomfortable.

Finally, my mom said, “now you understand how I feel when you guys talk to me about sex!”

Afterwards, I just put my baby in his car seat and packed everything up, and left. I have been crying and very distraught since.

Extra context; my boyfriend’s parents are extremely amazing people. We’ve never run into any problems while they babysat our child. When they do babysit, they frequently send check ins, photos, and videos. However with MY parents, it’s been very opposite. My mother fed our baby sour cream while babysitting when he was freshly 3 months old, after I repeatedly asked her not to give him anything other than milk. She has also taken him with her to a dispensary, which I was not okay with.

I have 4 siblings, which none of them have a good relationship with my parents.

My mother does have a past with sexual abuse (including when she was a child), which she refuses to go to therapy for. She instead smokes weed daily, which she says helps her more than anything.

When my mom said, “now you understand how I feel when you guys talk about sex!” I was extremely disturbed. Growing up, none of my siblings (3 sisters and 1 brother) couldn’t openly ask her anything that had to do with our anatomy. I never had the sex or period talk with my mother. The fact that she feels more okay with sexualizing my 4 month old by calling him a “pervert”, than answering her child’s questions about their bodies, worries me immensely.

The last time she did something that I didn’t agree with, I was about 7 months pregnant. She got so defensive, that she physically put her hands on me. Afterwards, she sat in a chair and sobbed and said “why do my kids treat me so horribly?” “I beat cancer and this is how I’m repaid?” “What did I do wrong?”

As of right now, I never want my child to go back over to my parents house. I don’t want him to have a relationship with my mother. She texted me asking if she is babysitting tomorrow, to which I nicely told her no. I am too scared to tell her how I really feel. But I plan to keep little to no contact, and absolutely no contact with my child.

Am I overreacting? Is this just some stupid joke?!
label: NOR

Maybe my question isn’t worded right but for context, my partner and I both have kids and we were talking about how they look the same size but one has more muscle mass therefore heavier than the other. His response was that yeah, his kid was built more like him and his kids’ mom and that they were more lean….and that my kid was built more like me and his dad and more stocky.

As a woman, being called “stocky” sounds like you’re telling me I’m built like a line backer and when I explained that “stocky” would mean that I have a wider build and although I do go to the gym, I’m not super muscular and I am definitely more of an hourglass shape but hold a little more in the tummy area. This made me feel like he was insinuating that I’m “stocky” because I hold more fat than him and his baby momma.

Am I overreacting? He did say sorry but I didn’t forgive him because I thoroughly explained what stocky meant as a woman coming from a man and he continued to say things like, “yeah, you always say your thighs are bigger than mine” , “that’s what you’re trying to do in the gym” , “you just look more muscular.”

I am about 5’4” and weigh 148 currently

I have muscle, yes, but I also have fat (yes I can see an acknowledge what I look like and where I tend to hold extra weight)

His baby momma was thin and went back to her “natural size” from what he said and is thicker now after having more kids but says it’s due to her lifestyle and not being active and/or maybe not being healthy

What was said in #3 made me think he thinks I’m not healthy enough because his baby momma and I both hold extra weight but are still “hourglass shape” so when asking if that’s what he’s essentially saying about me he says no, it’s probably just due to other things… ummm??? Okay..other things like what??? And his response was, “idk I’m just saying”

Maybe this is just me overreacting but again, my woman brain is going to view it more or less different than how he said and/or meant it. Just wondering if you’ve thought this or said it because no one has ever told me that I’m a “stocky” build so I immediately got defensive.
label: YOR

text:
Found out my girlfriend was texting a hookup 4 days after our first date. AIO?
I’ve been with my girlfriend for about a year and a half, everything is good between us. A few days ago I accidentally saw a screenshot on her phone from a guy she used to hook up with casually before me, where he was explicitly asking her to have sex. I connected the dots and realized this guy was still texting her on May 10th, four days after our first date. During those days we were texting each other non-stop, saying we missed each other, already giving each other nicknames — to me it was already clear where things were heading. Meanwhile on May 10th she was texting this guy saying she needed to hook up.

I know we weren’t official yet. I’ll also be honest — on our very first date I told her I wasn’t sure what I wanted, something serious or not. That was only the first day though, after that everything just naturally fell into place between us. She has always been extremely transparent with me — she’s told me about her past relationships and how many guys she’s been with, no secrets. So this isn’t a trust issue in general. But knowing this specific thing still makes me feel like shit.

Am I overreacting or is it understandable to feel this way?
label: YOR

text:
AIO for telling my husband that we need to teach our daughter to stop dragging other kids over?
I want to see if I’m NOR in being slightly annoyed that my husband doesn’t see an issue with what my daughter is doing.

My daughter is 3, will be 4 in two months.

With that being said, she is a VERY social person, but hasn’t been to anything like school or daycare so doesn’t interact with kids her age very much. Don’t worry, she starts school this year.

EVERY time we go to the park, she’ll find a kid she wants to play with and practically DRAGS them to me and my husband saying “come meet my parents, come say hi to my parents.”

To me, this is weird. Not saying my daughter has any ill will, again, she’s literally 3, but as a parent, if you know the signs of a kidnapper, you’d instantly freak out because that’s how people use other children to lure them to a blind spot.

It’s weird to me but when I got into my daughter about it at the park my husband was confused.

She’s never been taught this, she just does it.

Personally, if someone was doing this to my daughter I’d be super scared, but maybe it’s just me.

Am I overreacting or should I just let a kid be a kid?
label: YOR

text:
AIO - gf defends she loves me most to point of violence
Debated posting this for a while but I feel like this just can’t be normal. My gf (20) and I (17) have been dating for over a year and live together, we do all the normal things together, watch shows, go out to dinners, talk abt kinks, etc. But one thing that we do that a lot of others also do is play the I love you most game. In the beginning it was no more than light hearted fun and slowly overtime, our tactics got more complex as we learned about each others habits. However, recently she has been taking the game to new extremes. The first “incident” happened before we went to sleep. As I laid there and said “I love you most” she instantly rolled over and held a pillow over my face as she said she loved me most. I didn’t think anything of it because what’s a good game without a little suffocation? But to my surprise the next day in the shower, it happened again. I said the words and she shoved me into the water, shook shampoo off her head into my eyes and said “I love you most”. Now I was thinking this might be a little too far. But still I thought it might just be a bit of horseplay. Right? Wrong. Yesterday, as I hopped out of the car to go into the store I uttered the dreaded words. She instantly snapped her head around, shouted “No!” and threw the car into gear, tearing down the parking lot towards me as I ran and dove into the automatic store doors. Now I was terrified. It was apparent that she was willing to win by any lengths necessary and didn’t care about the repercussions to her or to my body. After this last incident I asked her why she took winning so seriously but she brushed it off as an over reaction to a game. Should I be concerned? Or is this a normal thing in relationships between competitive people?
label: YOR
