text:
AIO: Restaurant served me meat, I am vegetarian for spiritual reasons, my husband said it’s no big deal
Throwaway because my identity could be figured out in my other account. Tonight at a fast food drive thru I ordered a veggie black bean burger, I confirmed twice it was a veggie burger and the server also did. I did not explain that I have spiritual conflict with eating meat, I just trusted I would receive the veggie burger.

I drove off into the dark and began eating the burger in the way home. The burger had a lot of other toppings on it such as avocado, onion, jalepenos, so It wasn’t until I got in the house that I realized the texture wasn’t right. I opened the wrapper and it was clearly a meat burger. I called up the stairs to my husband what had happened and I told him I felt sick and upset and hoped I wouldn’t get sick. I gave the rest of the burger to my dog after removing the onion and jalapeño but I was very upset. I wrote to corporate to complain. Then I went upstairs and complained to my husband some more saying I wished I could throw up. He said it was no big deal and he’s gotten the “wrong order” at restaurants lots of times. He eats meat. I told him that it wasn’t the same because I am vegetarian for spiritual reasons and I felt dirty and gross eating this burger because I love cows and don’t want to hurt cows in any way. He blew up and said that I was making it a “big fucking deal” and complaining it about it endlessly (it was maybe 3 total minutes) and maybe he should go to a hotel. I have ptsd from a prior abusive relationship which he knows, so I got very upset and disregulated. I began to cry and went downstairs with the dog to sleep,

Am I over reacting?
label: YOR

text:
AIO for being angry my ex cheated on me with a dead guy?
Hey y'all. Bare with me because this is like...like I feel like a jerk for holding on to this kind of grudge, so maybe typing it out and getting another opinion will help me feel better. In my sophomore year of high school, I dated a girl that at the time, I felt like she was the love of my life. It was a short lived three months. She broke up with me. The reasoning at the time was that she felt I didn't care about our relationship because I didn't see her that much. I was grounded for getting bad grades one quarter and my punishment was not being able to see her, so that was on me (I called her every day after school which in hindsight I imagine must've been really annoying, so I wouldn't be surprised if breaking up with me was due to me doing what I thought was being a good boyfriend).

I was heartbroken for the first time, but life went on. Cut to our senior year, she and I give it another go. She'd spent her previous year being a partyer and she'd dated an older guy who had already graduated. I didn't care, I still ended up crazy about her again. We dated about 7 months up until before our graduation. We were both popular at school, with a bunch of friends. She was jealous of me having a few lady friends in my friend group and she would often accuse me of cheating on her, which personally I couldn't even imagine doing such a thing to her. I loved her so much that it was like no one else mattered. She never believed me and she told me that often, an example I can think of is, "you really don't love me like you say you do, and this isn't going to last." Ouchie.

I remember a specific winter night, she calls me and tells me a guy who graduated the year prior is harassing her on social media. She says he won't leave her alone and that he wants to hang out with her and that he wants her. This guy was a trademark piece of garbage. A lunkhead jock racist who had been known throughout the whole school for having been a drunk and physically abusive to his ex-girlfriend, who was in our senior class. I told her to block him and to stop talking to him, but she was really framing it like he wouldn't leave her alone. I don't recall her ever bringing him up again after that night.

We break up before graduation, she wasn't sure I was in it for the long run and she was moving out of state for college. We remain friends for a few years. A few more years go by and news goes around in our social media circle that the jock lunkhead guy dies in a car accident, he used to do all kinds of reckless driving. A lot of people from high school make social media posts grieving this guy, including my ex. She goes on to say "RIP, you were so charming. I'll never forget those late nights in high school where I would sneak out to be with you <3" Now y'all. I did the math. She was boo'ed up after me with a different guy, then boo'ed up with me again. I put two and two together and realized this chick was seeing this dude behind my back and was using the "he won't leave me alone" excuse in case she would've been caught! And what else would they be doing while they're out late at night and she's typing hearts in her post years later! And why that guy dammit! I was a mix of cracking up, shocked, pissed, and overall surprised because she was so against me having lady friends and she was so verbal about me not truly loving her! I had half a mind to throw it in her face and message her about it, but I long since decided to hold it in. Sometimes I still think about it and I have to wonder, AIO? How long do y'all hold grudges???

TLDR: High school ex was jealous when we dated, but cheated on me with a douche and I only found that out when he passed away years later and she made a social media post about how she used to go out with him during the timeline of her dating me and I'm not seething about it, but like reflective/nostalgic/hindsight mad about it and the silly waste of time it was. I'm over it, I just thought it would be entertaining and fun to type out and discuss.
label: YOR

text:
AIO for getting mad at my boyfriend for getting drunk at my birthday when he was meant to drive us home
Hi all, tonight was my birthday. And my boyfriend of 8 yrs organised a dinner for me with friends.

Before we left for dinner we discussed driving vs uber and decided that it wouldn’t be a big night and he would be designated driver so I could enjoy a few drinks.

We ended up heading out early to catch up with a friend before dinner for a few drinks. At this point his friend offered my boyfriend his weed vape, which my boyfriend declined and said he’s driving.

Fast forward an hour and my boyfriend is on his 3rd or 4th pint of beer and I’m still on my first, he turns to me and says am I still driving or are you? I responded with well it was meant to be you but I’m not sure how that’s going to work now. And he’s like we can uber. I said I’d rather not leave my car parked in some alley way in the city over night and have to sort coming back to get it. He nodded but kept sipping away.

Few minutes later he grabs the weed vape from his friend and is like to me “you don’t mind do you” and took a big puff, I was shocked…. We live in an area very strict on drug driving and he needs his license for work so in that moment I knew he can’t drive home. I was angry and just said “you do want you want” and he took more puffs. I was upset, he completely changed the plan. And I felt he didn’t really care that it was my birthday and consider maybe I wanted to have some drinks.

After we were walking to the restaurant, he’s like “are you okay, you’re not mad are you?” and I said I am mad. I said what he chose to do was selfish, he put me on the spot and changed the plan to suit his own wants without considering what I wanted on my birthday. He then apologised and kept saying leave the car we’ll get an uber. I said we should have done that from the start if he wanted to drink(etc) tonight, most of our friends are driving, he and the other friend who gave him the weed are the only ones getting drunk at this point. Anyways for the sake of dinner I said it’s fine and move on. I had one drink at dinner and drove us home, he probably had another 4 drinks. Once we got home he said to me that I can’t be upset because there were other options and I chose not to have a good time. I told him that he can say whatever it is to make himself feel better, but we have an agreement and he changed it last minute to suit himself… on my birthday. It’s fair I didn’t want to leave my car parked over night in a dodgy area of the city, so yes I chose to drive home.

I’m so angry at the situation and now he’s not taking accountability. He isn’t normally like this. I need some time and I’m taking some space sleeping in the spare room, do you think I’m over reacting?

Edit: please be kind, I’m feeling super shit rn, I don’t need mean comments.
label: NOR

text:

She'd told me he was trying to get back with her, but I trusted her and certainly wasn't going to control who she can and can't work with.

One day I look at her socials and see him post "Thanks for the chocolates, love heart".

I'm like errr my girlfriend is buying her ex, who is still into her, chocolates.

I was like sorry but I'm not comfortable with this, and broke it off.

She then explains she had brought them to say thank you to him for switching shifts.

EDIT: Even with that explanation I still felt uncomfortable, because I would have appreciated a heads up along the lines of hey he wrote this comment and I realize it could look bad out of context, but here's the explanation. Instead she said nothing to me and I came across it days later. I explained this and her response was, do you want me to delete social media? I felt perplexed by this because I'm not controlling like that, and have no issue with other guys talking to her on social media whatsoever. I didn't want to get into a cyclical argument for hours and felt I wasn't being heard, so said agree to disagree and ended it.

Did I overreact?

text:
AIO for being upset that my engaged friend told her childhood admirer she "regrets saying no" to him?
My friend Susy (40F) and I were at a party recently with some friends, including Mark (40M). For context, Susy and Mark have a bit of history. When they were teenagers, Mark was totally in love with her. He confessed his feelings back then, but she rejected him. It was not in a bad way but I don’t think he’s ever fully gotten over her.

Fast forward to today: Susy is engaged to be married later this year. She and her soon to be husband have a daughter together, and she has always seemed content about her upcoming wedding.

During the party, Susy and Mark went outside for a few minutes to talk. When they came back inside, Mark looked noticeably shaken. Shortly after, Susy left the party to go pick up her daughter. Since Mark still looked concerned, I pulled him aside and asked if he was okay.

That’s when he told me what happened outside. He said that Susy brought up their past and told him she regrets saying no to his attentions all those years ago.

I was very surprised and, honestly, upset by this. I might be overreacting but it feels something pretty disrespectful to say, especially since she is getting married in a few months and there is no actual future for her and Mark. I don't understand why she would say that to him now. I guess it's a nostalgia trip, but if anything her timing seems very off.

When I talked to my boyfriend about what happened, he brushed it off. He thinks there is nothing wrong with what she said, that it was actually nice of her, but I feel like if I were her fiancé, I would be mad and feel somewhat betrayed.

I mean, she's getting married real soon, shouldn't she be overjoyed by the thought of the wedding? Shouldn't she be all over her future husband?

I don't know why but something seems off and it’s been rubbing me the wrong way. Am I overreacting?

EDIT I see a lot of YOR just because it's not my business. I know and I get this, in fact I never meant to directly meddle in their affairs. What I meant to ask Reddit's opinion on is if I am wrong in my negative first reaction to what she said. Is my feeling that Susy was inappropriate out of touch with reality or maybe there's a pov that I'm missing?

Thank you all for your feedback though and sorry if I was unclear in my request.
label: YOR

text:
AIO by telling my boyfriend he can’t go on vacation to a place we’ve been wanting to go together our entire relationship?
Now I know most people reading the title probably think I am overreacting, but there’s more to the situation than just that. My boyfriend has been working through some tough personal issues lately, and we’ve been having relationship problems recently as well (more on that later). He recently lost his job, so he wants to go on a trip to Yellowstone for a week now that he has all this free time to clear his head and work on himself and the issues he/we are having. Now I’m not a crazy, possessive girlfriend. I like to do my own things with my friends without him often and he does his too, so the problem is not about him going on a trip without me (although I will admit, part of me is salty that I have to go to work while he can just frolic in the flowers, but again im not the type of person to tell him he can’t just because I can’t, I wouldn’t want him to do that to me, I’m very supportive and he has told me that before.) The problem is the location. We have talked about going to Yellowstone together since we met (over a year ago) we almost went last year but decided to go to Europe instead. We talk about it pretty often and he has already been there multiple times so he knows that it’s something important that we wanna do together. text:
AIO for being upset that my partner abandoned a meal they were cooking me?
For context, I (31M) was up until 4 in the morning last night with my girlfriend (not the partner I live with, I am polyamorous) because I took her to the ER. I got about six hours of sleep, had to work a full day on my feet today, and didn’t have time or money to get lunch. Once I got home, I asked my nesting partner (31NB) if they’d be willing to cook me dinner, which they agreed to. I realized there were a couple things I wanted in my food that we didn’t have so I headed to the store to grab them. Meanwhile, my partner started cooking and by the time I got home they weren’t there. I got confused and called them, and they said they’d gone to a different store to get soda (??) before the store closed. I reacted a bit incredulously, I will admit, not angry, but I was definitely surprised that they’d just left in the middle of cooking. Heat was not left on so I wasn’t worried anything was dangerous just a bit frustrated that they’d hadn’t taken me into consideration before just leaving. I ended up cooking the rest of the meal by myself since I was hungry and figured I’d rather my feet hurt and be tired than wait a whole lot longer.

Now here’s my thing. If I was cooking a meal for a partner who was tired and hungry, and realized there was something I wanted with a time limit on it, I would have called to ask if my partner would be back soon and if they’d be okay with me leaving it for 20 minutes to do said other task. I brought this up to them and they said they were sorry for asking for my forgiveness instead of my permission. (This was the first time they’d said the words I’m sorry to me over this). I very politely explained to them why this was upsetting to me and I’ve gotten essentially no empathy in return. It feels to me like my needs and what my partner agreed to do for me were just left by the wayside for a fairly unimportant task.

AIO for being upset that they didn’t communicate with me and abandoned a meal they were cooking partway through?

Edit to clarify some things: Yes I am also confused that they didn’t call to ask me to pick up soda. Like I said originally, I wasn’t angry so much as I was confused by their behavior. The things I left to grab were broccoli and mushrooms, which don’t take as long to cook as a lot of the rest, plus we are about a 4 minute drive from the store I headed to so I wasn’t far. They left to go to a much farther store for some reason even though there are sodas they like from the store I was already heading to that they could have asked me to grab. I’m mostly hurt that they did not communicate and literally left something they said they’d do for me on the back burner while going to do something relatively unimportant or that I could have easily done while I was out. For those that are wondering, I work in the service industry and it is common for me or my coworkers or anyone working a similar job to skip breaks when it’s busy (like it was today) because if you take a break you will be missing out on not just wages but tips. I have a 10-year old so every cent counts. I would also like to mention that my nesting partner and I have been having a lot of conversations about evening out the work load at home because I end up taking a lot more of it on than they do even though I work more hours than they do (they have agreed with me on this). So they have been saying they will try to do more especially on the days that I work and they don’t, and this just felt like the opposite of what we’d been talking about, which is part of why it was a little frustrating.

I also want y’all to know that the way y’all are treating it like it is a straight relationship is kind of funny to me. My partner is a male-bodied enby and I am AFAB transmasculine enby. I’m not some cis dude expecting to be waited on hand and foot by my live-in gf or whatever you people are expecting lol.

Update for anyone interested: My partner and I had a good long conversation about this. They ended up admitting that it is difficult for them to ask for things from other people and that’s why they just left to go get the soda themselves instead of calling to ask me. I have been through a lot of therapy over the years and it has gotten much easier for me to ask for things, and I expressed that it makes it harder for me to feel like I should ask for those things if they are treated in this way (there are other examples of this happening in our relationship, such as household tasks and things relating to the child). They understood and did apologize for not communicating with me, which was the basis of why I was feeling upset. I told them I want them to trust me enough to know they can communicate with me about things like this so we can figure something out that works better for us, such as me grabbing sodas for them while I’m already out. They agreed and we are on much better ground now.

For all of you that think I overreacted, I want you all to reread my post where I explicitly mentioned not being angry at my partner but just expressing how their actions had hurt me. Such communication is necessary in a relationship and I hope that all of you can attempt to express your feelings and resolve things genuinely in your own relationships. Peace out y’all.
label: YOR

text:
AIO for going NC with my family after they ate dinner without us?
I (42 F) and my family, husband (39 M) 3 daughters (11,8,6) were invited to my parents for Thanksgiving. Back in October my mother sent me a text message asking if we would like to come to Thanksgiving. We have not been in several years as we have not been invited. I reply yes of course! What time should we be there?

My mother replies back dinner will be at five show up just a few minutes before five.

I thought that was a bit odd, as they would not want to visit with us before but figured OK. We’re at least being invited.

Thanksgiving week rolls around, I reach out to my mother asking her if she would like us to bring anything. She replies nope we have everything covered….

Thanksgiving day rolls around. We hang around the house have a few snacks and enjoy the day together. At 4 o’clock that afternoon, my mother sends me a text message.: are you on your way?

I reply no, you told us to be there a few minutes before five.

I do not receive anything back from her. We only live about 10 minutes from my parents so around 4:30. We decide to leave a few minutes early and get there to see the family. Knowing that my brother, his wife has two kids and my aunt would also be there, including my parents.

When we arrive. I placed the dish that I did make down on the table excitingly saying “Happy Thanksgiving!”to everybody. I look around everyone is sitting at the table and then I noticed there is a turkey carcass and a half eaten bowl of mashed potatoes. I say, “oh, you already ate.”

I also noticed that there was no chairs for us to sit down. My children are standing in the kitchen and my husband is around the corner looking very, very mad.. i’m still just a bit confused at this point. My aunt turns to me in a very snarky way and states “oh I guess you didn’t get the memo about when dinner was.”

I reply back “yes we did. We were told several times dinner was at five. “

No one else says anything to us. They all just kind of looked out at their plates. My mom, then acts like nothing is wrong and calls the youngest of my children to come sit on her lap. Then proceeds to tell my father to get the things out of the refrigerator and we can have dinner.

I state “we do not want leftovers for Thanksgiving no thank you.”

my husband is still around the corner looking very upset. At this point, I am pretty pissed off too. I find a chair in the corner and sit down. Do not say anything to anybody and just sit there and let my mom visit with my children.

After a few minutes my aunt and dad start speaking to one another and quiet tones. All I hear is “wow what an attitude she has.”

I ask..Are you talking about my attitude?”

Note-I have not stated anything after I said no thank you we do not want leftovers for Thanksgiving. I am quietly sitting in the corner seething.

My aunt spins around and rudely says “yes, your attitude! you’re being very rude!”

I stand up” you bet your ass I have a fucking attitude. You’re rude. This entire family is rude. This is so disrespectful to our kids to do this.”

Some more yelling proceeds. My husband, then steps in and calmly asks if this happened to you would you be upset? My aunt replies yes of course I would be upset! (note these people have always been the type that they feel they can do whatever they want and that is always right however, if it’s something happens to them in the same way, it is not allowed or appropriate basically they’re hypocrites)

My husband says “let’s go. We’re leaving.”

No apologies have ever been made to us and we have not spoken to my family since. My mother sent me text message about how horrible I am to keep her grandchildren from her as she did nothing wrong….

A little bit of context about this is the straw that broke the camels back type of situation. My parents have always been very disrespectful to me and my husband throughout the last 15 years. Think lots of gaslighting, lying, even announcing our pregnancies when asked not to. We always just shrugged it off as we are adults and they are my parents and you’re supposed to do that sort of thing with your parents, but this time they were disrespectful to our children and I do not want my children growing up with grandparents that treat them as such..

So, am I overreacting? I do not wish to speak to my family. I am simply over it. I’m over the disrespect. I’m over the gaslighting. I’m over the lying.
label: NOR

text:
AIO My husband called our toddler an idiot and I can’t get over it.
Our daughter (3F) was whining a few nights ago because it was bedtime and she didn’t want to go. While doing this she was doing what I believe are normal toddler things (rolling away, somersaulting, etc…just “I’m 3 and can’t stay still” stuff) and then walked on top of our iPad, which was screen up on the floor. My husband gasped and said “get off it you…idiot!” There was a long pause between the last 2 words, as I think he was intending to say something worse and then stopped himself. 3F had never heard that word before so she didn’t really react, but I did. I grabbed her into a hug and angrily told my husband “no! We are not calling our toddler an idiot!” Then I turned to her and said “Daddy should not have called you a mean name and I’m sorry about that.” He mumbled an apology. I took 3F to get ready for bed.

I later texted him while with 3F in the bedroom that he should come apologize to her. He did and said “I’m sorry, I shouldn’t have called you a mean name.” 3F said it was okay and snuggled right up to him to read a bedtime story. She seemed largely unaffected by the whole thing.

It’s days later, though, and I still can’t get over it. Of course most toddler parents have had rough thoughts about their child (they are expert button pushers!) but even on my worst day, I’d never verbalize it. He told me he thought she was purposely trying to break the iPad. I don’t think she has that capacity at her age but even if she did, I don’t think calling her names is appropriate. I can’t believe he would do that. He’s tried to initiate sex a few times since then and I just can’t imagine being intimate with a man who would treat our daughter like this. It’s like something broke in me.

AIO?

ETA: as suggested by multiple commenters I’m adding the following information: husband’s been seen in therapy a couple times for anger issues but did not continue due to work schedule and, I suspect, incompatibility with the therapist.

ETA part 2: thanks everyone for weighing in. It seemed like the reactions were pretty split down the middle between YOR and NOR which…huh. Wasn’t really sure how to proceed. Anyway, had a good talk with husband today about how I felt him calling her that wasn’t appropriate and I also apologized for the way I reacted. He was initially defensive, saying “it’s way better than the way I was raised” which might be true but should not be our measuring stick for parenting, as I reminded him. He agreed to consider trying therapy again, I said I wouldn’t force the issue unless this happens again. Anyway, thanks for all the feedback. To those who were rude, hope you step on a Lego <3

text:
AIO - Popsicles at a party
Am I overreacting to be irritated at this behavior?

Birthday party - It's a large park but a majority of the guests are in one picnic area partitioned off from the rest of the park. Approximately 15 kids ages 4 - 6. A small handful of kids older.

An ice cream vendor with a small cart is doing slow circles around the entire park.

Grandma buys herself a popsicle, sits in front of all the kids and eats it. My 10 year old whines in that big kid way and says once, "aw, I wanna popsicle..." But there's no real passion behind it, he isn't like, upset or throwing a fit. It was just a momentary pout. But grandma says, "oh, here's a couple bucks, go buy yourself one!" Kid looks around and is like uhhh, I don't think my parents will---- but she insists and ropes in another adult to walk over to the ice cream person who had already walked away and buys him a popsicle. I wasn't there at the moment but saw the 2 of them walking back to the picnic area with 1 popsicle.

I was like, "uh, what are you doing?" And he's like, "they bought it for me!" And I looked at his uncle and was like wtf. He shrugs and is like, grandma's orders! So I said, well grandma's orders can go eat a popsicle over there cause you can't walk into the middle of a kids party with ONE popsicle. So he stood off to the side in the shade to eat his popsicle. He wasn't completely ostracized and I didn't take it away or anything like that. There was a shady area where some of the adults were hanging out across the path and not in the main center of picnic tables. He also wasn't punished or admonished.

AIO for thinking it's rude and bad etiquette to get yourself and one kid a popsicle when:

you can't buy all the kids a popsicle

not all the kid's parents can buy their kid a popsicle

AND everyone can see you and must watch you eat said popsicle?

Things to consider: 5 year olds are horrible at filtering their envy, understanding why they get some and they do not, and waiting. Cupcakes and cake AND candies were coming in the next half hour or so. There were also juice boxes and sodas.

I don't know if I'm overreacting because me and my family were poor and often didn't get extra sweets. when we did my parents always emphasized it had to be enough for everyone and to leave no one out. My MIL (and her children) have never experienced not having enough. They've always had their needs met. Not to say they had a perfect life, just that it is a particular struggle to not have enough food and gives a different perspective on things like sharing.

My partner wants nothing to do with the ex besides a healthy co-parenting relationship, which we all have, luckily.

My issues is the Parents and the ex. They refuse to cut contact to a reasonable amount. They invite her out to dinner and events, just her (and usually their grandson too). They show up at her house unannounced and do chores for her. They treat her like an angel, and pretend that they don't remember how poorly she treated their son.

He has expressed multiple times that it bothers him. Together we have also expressed multiple times that it bothers us as a couple. We want our boundaries respected. We understand that they want to spend time with their grandson, and do not want to limit that at all, he loves spending time with them as well.

However, they crossed our boundary again, and showed up at the ex's place again, and again tried to keep it from us. We found out when we had a group parents call with the ex about the child's struggles in school.

Myself, my partner, his son, and his parents had plans this Friday. I do not want to go, and am pretty adamant that I will not be attending. I told my partner I prefer he doesn't go as well, but understand if he wants to. (He has always had a strained relationship with his father, and has always felt emotionally detached from him. His stepmother has also treated him poorly the majority of his life).

AIO for refusing to attend and asking him not to go as well?

TLDR: Partner's parents try to maintain close friendship with his ex, when him and I have both expressed extreme discomfort with it. They spent alone time with her again, and I am choosing to not attend an event with them now. AIO for not attending and asking my partner not to go as well?

EDIT:

Thank you to all the thoughtful responses! Partner and I have talked it all through, and I will be passing on this event (as i'm not emotionally ready and will definitely have RBF). Moving forward, I will smile and nod at future gatherings. We have also decided that personal and vulnerable information will not be shared with grandparents because of the lack of trust.
label: YOR

text:
AIO after my best friend’s husband secretly snuck a flash drive into my purse?
I (31F) need a sanity check because I genuinely can’t tell if I’m making this into a bigger deal than it is.
My best friend “Carly” (32F) is married to “Nick” (34M). I’ve honestly never been comfortable around him because he’s crossed boundaries with me multiple times over the years.

Some examples:
- Sat next to me on the couch and started stroking my arm.
- Came up behind me and given me “head scratches.”
Smacked my hip/butt “for emphasis” while he was talking (I don’t even remember what he was saying because it caught me so off guard)
- Randomly texts me for the weirdest reasons. Most recently he asked me, “How do bounce dresses work?” Like ????? Do I look like Google????
- Makes lots of comments about me receiving “pretty privilege”
- Randomly takes “candid photos” of me (he does this to other people too, but half the time he doesn’t even share them with whomever they’re of later, and this makes me super uncomfy regardless)

I’ve explicitly told him I don’t like being touched, and that I would prefer to keep our friendship within the context of Carly, because he is HER husband, not my friend. Despite that, the last time I saw him he grabbed/tickled my side just to startle me.

The latest incident is what’s really throwing me though. Apparently while we were all hanging out, he somehow unzipped my purse without me noticing and snuck a flash drive inside it. He didn’t tell me. I found it later after I got home.
When I asked about it, I was told it was anime he thought I’d think was “cute” or “funny.” For context, I don’t even watch anime.
My partner works in IT so he looked at it for me. There was a folder labeled “Start Here,” and the first manga was called “There’s No Freaking Way I’ll Be Your Lover! Unless”

In the past when there have been weird incidents, Carly has suggested that Nick is just “on the spectrum” and “doesn’t understand social cues.” He isn’t diagnosed with anything. He was also in a fraternity in college and works as a CPA, just to paint more of a picture of who he is. I mention this only to point out that he doesn’t struggle ENOUGH socially to prevent him from living a relatively normal life, despite struggling to make friends.

For some additional context, I identified as a lesbian for several years before recently realizing I’m pansexual and started dating a man. I have no idea if that title was supposed to be some kind of joke because of that, but even if it was… who thinks that’s appropriate?

Honestly, the title almost bothers me less than the fact that he secretly went into my zipped purse in the first place. If he wanted to recommend something, why not text me a link like a normal person? Or ask Carly to send it to me? Why sneak something into my purse without telling me?

Carly knows I don’t really like her husband (for a lot of reasons beyond this), and she also knows I’ve had conversations with him about respecting my physical boundaries.

I just feel icky and violated by the whole thing, but I also still have doubts in my mind that he really is just trying to be nice and he is just completely socially clueless :(

I did reach out to her yesterday to tell her about the contents of the flash drive and how it made me feel.

Is this as weird as it feels? Or am I overreacting?
label: NOR

text:
AIO about my girlfriend’s naps?
My girlfriend and I have been together for a year this month and hang out at least 3 times a week. Every time she comes over to my house since we’ve been together, she takes a 2-3 hour long nap and sometimes even longer, and then after she wakes up she goes on her phone and it’s impossible to get her attention. She’ll leave shortly after that to either go home or go hang out with another friend. Before she takes a nap, she goes on her phone or turns over and again it’s impossible to get her attention. She knows that it’s really started to bother me because she will come over to my house and just sleep for hours, making it impossible to spend any time with her or do things together. She complains she gets bored being at my house all the time but I’m 17, she’s 18, and I don’t yet have a car and nobody can take us anywhere. She’s constantly going out with other friends to eat, drive places, and do things, and she’ll leave our hang out nearly every single time to go do something with another friend, meanwhile she’ll come to my house, sleep, and scroll on her phone. I asked her why this is and she gave me no answer other than she’s sorry. She complains about me “bitching” about how all she does is sleep when she’s at my house and that it’s not a big deal because she sees me so often. It feels like I’m not actually hanging out with her though because all she ever does is nap. She thinks it’s funny and laughs while I’m trying to tell her how it makes me feel because she (in her words) can’t take me serious. She tells me I’m making a big deal out of nothing but it’s to the point I don’t want her coming over anymore. She never wants to do anything or walk anywhere, she won’t even come hang out with me in the kitchen when I’m baking. All she wants to do is lay in bed, which is seriously boring. The whole point of hanging out is spending time with someone, at least to me, and no time is spent together when it solely consists of scrolling on our phones and going to sleep and sitting in basically silence. I understand not being in a talkative mood or being tired sometimes, but this is a constant occurrence and it’s honestly exhausting. I feel bad because I know she can’t help being tired, but she does a million things with everyone else which I’m never included in, which is mostly fine because I want her to have separate time with her friends but then she comes back and just doesn’t ever want to do anything. She asked to hangout out today, and while I do want to see her, I didn’t want her coming over because she just would’ve ended up going to sleep. Am I overreacting and is it really not a big deal like she says, or am I right about it being a bother?

text:
AIO to my boyfriend actively rejecting my intimacy advances
Hello everyone,

I (29F) have a boyfriend (32M)of 2½ years.

We have been living together for the past 1½ years approximately. And as we have had our ups and downs one thing that I think I just keep resenting on him is the lack of sex life.

I think I am an active person on that matter, I like to have it a lot and more when I love and feel comfortable with the person. I have never cheated on him, we have discussed that would be a deal breaker for our relationship, but also I don't know what to do anymore here.

I know that with time sex become less frequent and, well, life sometimes just doesn't align with it, but I feel like this started like really early on on our relationship.

At first I thought it was very passionate and every time we had the chance we would take it to be intimate. I didn't live in the same city as him, so maybe it was because we weren't seeing each other as often. Then I moved to the same city but lived with roommates then we used to maybe at least have intimacy twice a week (the days we saw each other as we still didn't live together) but I felt him slowly drifting apart and that when I tried to kiss him or cuddle him he wouldn't even look at me and just kinda seemed like he was a bit disgusted. I felt really bad about it and was thinking maybe it was my physical appearance that he didn't like anymore (I also thought this because he really insisted for me to go to the gym with him, made some fatphobic comments and so) I'm not skinny and I struggle a lot trying to lose weight, but I also think I'm not obese. (Like a size 10 US).

We talked about it and he assured me it wasn't me, he still liked me and loved me and he only wanted me to exercise for my own health (but he was being really pushy about it) so I asked him, what was it then? He didn't tell me, he said he didn't know and that he just couldn't get it on. That having almost no sex was his normal because before me he would have it like once or twice a year if he didn't have a girlfriend, and even then they didn't last. And that (I feel like he kinda slut shamed me here) because he knew I was more active than him he thought it was all I could think of or all that I cared about. (Something like that he said, I don't remember exact words as it was a long time ago).

But I remember it made me feel really bad, and ever since he is constantly making me feel like I'm harassing him sexually or something. He already established some boundaries (he doesn't like him being too touched and that he wants space for intimacy) and I have been maintaining those but I can't help to feel very lonely and frustrated. He doesn't even like it when I try to initiate things, he didn't like it when I was being indirect, like starting to cuddling or kissing, he also doesn't like that I straight forward tell him "I want to have sex" I asked him why but he just said he doesn't like it.

So I feel very trapped, I can't ask for what I want but also can't go and try to get it.

And it's not that I feel entitled to have sex every time I want to do it, because I know that's not real, but I would like to have a mid point. And I have told him that, that we can only kiss if he doesn't feel like it, but I still feel like he doesn't even want to kiss me.

I feel also that is unfair that when it happens it's when he wants it and I just have to be like "well, if not now when?" Even if I don't totally feel like it.

Sooo, AIO here? Am I asking for too much on a relationship? Is there any advices you guys have. I literally don't know what to do or how to feel at this point.
label: NOR

text:
AIO for telling my adoptive mom I won’t attend her bio son’s wedding
Hey yall, I’m 20F and I was adopted when I was 6 after a rough start. My adoptive mom (52F) has two biological kids: my older “brother” (25M) and younger sister (18F). They’ve always treated me decently, at least the women the problem always is her son who I will call Mark. My mom tries hard to make us one big happy family, but my brother especially has this subtle (not at all) way of reminding me I’m not real family.

It started small, he would always introduce me as “the adopted sister” to his friends (unnecessarily), make sure to always try and spend time with everyone else but me, make dumbass jokes about how I came with paperwork, and a bunch of other stuff I wasn’t a fan of but I brushed it off because I didn’t want to seem ungrateful. My mom always says “blood or not, family is family” and kinda guilt trips me into letting things slide even if she doesn’t mean to.

Last year for Mom’s birthday, they took a big family photo… without me. I was literally in the house helping set up. When I asked why, my brother said “It was just the original kids with Mom, no big deal.” Mom made him add me in a separate shot later, but it felt forced.

Now he’s getting married in three months to his long-term girlfriend. I’m supposed to be a bridesmaid (my Mom wanted me to, not him), but he’s been gatekeeping everything. The engagement party? “Cousins and blood family only” which I found out through IG. Wedding planning group chat? I’m not in it. When I confronted him, he said I’m fucking overreacting and that the wedding is “small and intimate for close family.” Mom keeps saying he’ll come around and I should just be the bigger person.

Last weekend, I decided I was gonna take a stand finally. Mom was pressuring me to go dress shopping with the bridal party (which includes his fiancée’s sisters but somehow not me in the real planning). I told her straight up that I’m done and If he can’t treat me like a real sister, I won’t stand up there smiling in photos or pretend everything’s fine. I said I’d skip the wedding entirely if it means being excluded again.

Mom started crying, saying I’m tearing the family apart and that I’m somehow punishing her for my brother’s “awkwardness,” and that at 20 I should be more mature about this. My sister is staying mostly neutral but also texting me that I’m making it “all about me.” My brother sent a passive message like “Sorry you feel that way, hope you change your mind.”

Part of me feels guilty because my Mom has done a lot for me, and I know weddings are stressful. But the other part is exhausted from years of feeling like an outsider in my own home. I love my adoptive family, but I can’t keep faking it while being sidelined.
AIO for drawing this line? Or is it fair to protect my peace even if it hurts my Mom? I still live at home with her while in college, so makes it a little more awkward.
label: NOR

text:
AIO for getting into an argument with a mom and her kid at a bowling alley?
I’m a 29 year old guy and last weekend I went bowling by myself. I actually take bowling kind of seriously and usually go to relax. The lane next to me ended up being a mom and her son who looked maybe 6 years old.
At first I could tell it was going to be one of those situations. The kid had bumpers up and one of those little ramps they roll the ball down. Every frame took forever because he would stand there deciding which ball he wanted, then the mom would encourage him, then he would roll it and they'd both celebrate no matter what happened.
The thing is, the kid honestly wasn't being bad. He wasn't running all over the place or screaming. He was just being a little kid. But the constant cheering and talking was getting on my nerves. Every time he knocked down a few pins the mom acted like he had won the Super Bowl.
I was already irritated and then at one point while I was getting ready for my turn, the kid wandered a few feet into my approach area. He wasn't even really in my way. He just kind of stepped over the line and looked down my lane for a second.
His mom immediately called him back and he listened right away.
For some reason that set me off.
I turned to her and said, "Can you control it?"
She looked confused and said, "Control who?"
I said, "Your kid."
She immediately got angry and told me not to call her son "it."
I told her I didn't care what term I used and that I was tired of dealing with distractions every time I came up to bowl.
She said her son was six years old and had stepped a few feet in the wrong direction for about two seconds.
I said that wasn't the point and that people come there to bowl, not listen to parents clap and cheer all afternoon.
The kid got really quiet after that and just stood next to her.
The mom told me I was being unbelievably rude and that if I wanted complete silence I should build a bowling lane in my house.
I told her maybe parents should stop bringing kids everywhere if they can't keep them from bothering other people. I left soon after but still am a bit annoyed by the whole thing when I was the one minding my own business. So AITAH for this entire ordeal?
label: YOR

text:
AIO for questioning how much of the top pool my manager takes?
So I just started at a cafe about a month ago and when the manager (24M, manager for 4 years) hired me he told me tips were $3 per hour and they pool the rest for winter when tips drop lower. I thought that was a bit strange but took the job because I needed one.

I asked my coworker about the tip situation and she said he had told her something different, that it was just a direct split. I then asked him about it again and he said they weren’t doing it anymore. I then asked for a tip pool breakdown, and he kept saying that he would get me that information and then wouldn’t follow through.

I decided to add up the tips myself on the POS which he saw me doing on the cameras and called the cafe to tell me to stop. He said he’d go through the breakdown with me the next day, but then didn’t show up, and when I texted him near the end of my shift he said he wouldn’t be getting there until after my shift was over. I said I could wait, and he showed up about an hour after my shift, I asked for just the hourly tip breakdown because he claimed that the total amount of tips was just divided by the total hours, but he couldn’t give me those exact numbers, instead just started showing me random excel sheets with random numbers saying that only the accountant had that info.

I kept asking for just the tip breakdown which he still couldn’t give me directly. He showed me that the hours are wayy more than what’s on the schedule because:

He tips himself out for 40 hours of on-the-floor shift work per week and then he gets another 12 hours of managerial pay on top of that, didn’t say how much that is.
The wife of the owner comes in at night to bake at night, apparently 18 hours per week but we don’t even have that many baked goods.
The son of the owner randomly comes in whenever he chooses, he’s 17 and in high school, but none of them are on the schedule nor clock in.

The fact that he can’t give me straight answers, the untracked hours, the lack of tip breakdown. It just seems weird to me. I’ve also been working 40 hours per week and have not seen him here for that many hours.

It might be worth noting that the owners don’t speak English- they’re Persian, and only the son and the manager (who is also Persian) can speak English.

Now the manager is talking shit to the other employees who are his girlfriend, his best friend and his roommate – the only ones that are consistent employees otherwise there’s a lot of turnover.

The whole thing just feels weird and immature and unnecessary and I’m not sure I want to work here anymore, am I overreacting??

TLDR: I work at a cafe in British Colombia, manager won’t give me a straight answer about the tip breakdown, pays himself tips for 40 hours per week of on the floor work though he’s not doing the same amount that we are and he gets paid an additional 12 hours of “manager wage” but didn’t disclose how much that is.

text:
AIO for wanting to end things because of a rental car and speeding?
Context: I (31F) have been dating someone (37M) for about 2 months and we are planning our first trip together. It's a road trip about 2 hours from where we live. We will be camping 2 nights and then staying at a hotel for 2 nights. The hotel location is high-end area.

We both do not own cars since we live in a walkable city, and owning a car is more of a headache than it's worth. He offered to rent a car for the trip and suggested a sports car/convertible. I politely told him I am not a fan of convertibles, as I don't even like driving with the windows down. He laughed about it and said he will still find something nice. I told him no need, as we will be camping half the time and something practical will do. He pushed back a little and said it would be fun to get something nice, since our second destination (hotel) has a "car scene".

Issue: Today he sent me a screenshot of the car rental reservation. It was a convertible that retails for 6-figures. In my opinion, flashy and impractical. While this may be someone else's dream, it's just not my thing. I replied "haha, that's funny. I'll take the bus and meet you there". He replied, "you were actually serious about no convertibles?"

I told him I appreciate the gesture and thanked him arranging the car rental. I also asked if car rental could be changed. I reiterated that I am not a fan of top/windows down, nor flashy cars. He said he understood, but thought it would be so fun to speed down the highway with the wind.

I told him that made me nervous since I have strong opinions about speeding. Unfortunately, I've had a close friend and acquaintance lose their lives due to speeding accidents. He replied, "it's so fun though, I love it! You shouldn't knock it til you try it. There's no better rush!" I explained why I felt strongly about it and he immediately apologized and promised not to speed while I was in a car with him.

His initial response really has me questioning everything. I didn't love being asked about my car preferences and then ignored, but the speeding comment really bothers me (despite his apology). AIO for wanting to end something over this?

text:
AIO for wanting my partner to distance himself from his family?

My partner (33M) and I (26F) have been together for almost four years. We are long distance (Canada and US) and generally have a loving relationship where we are one another's best friend, but his family has become a major issue.

When I first met his family, I genuinely tried to make a good impression. I brought a bottle of wine as a gift, was polite, and cleaned up after myself and my partner during the visit.

What made the situation so confusing for me is that his mother made very little effort to actually get to know me. After meeting me only once, she decided she did not want me over again, nor attending any events she hosted in the future. Later, reasons given for disliking me included things such as my hair color, clothes, tattoos, lack of religion, and the fact that I did not offer to help in the kitchen during that first visit.

To be clear, I was never asked for help, and at the time I thought I was behaving appropriately as a guest in someone else's home. It left me feeling like I had been judged and rejected before I was ever given a real chance. When my partner told her that I wanted to get to know her better, she responded with "is there a reason I need to get to know her?"

Another source of tension has been my health. I have a disability and was upfront about my physical limitations from the beginning. During visits, there were times when plans were slightly adjusted because I was experiencing symptoms or couldn't physically keep up with what everyone else wanted to do.

I attended these events because my partner wanted me there, despite already knowing that most family members disliked me and spoke negatively about me. Instead of viewing those situations as health-related limitations, his mother and sister later characterized me as rude and/or lazy for inconveniencing them. That was particularly painful because I was already pushing myself beyond my comfort zone to participate and spend time with them.

Since then, things have only gotten worse.

His mother has openly stated that she doesn't want anything to do with me, and stated that the rest of the family feels the same. At one point I attended a gathering because my partner wanted all of his loved ones together despite my reservations. Afterward, I was cropped out of the family photos. This happened shortly after my grandmother's funeral. When confronted, his mother later said she didn't care that it hurt me.

I've also been blamed for problems I didn't create. His family has accused me of making no effort to get along with them, despite the fact that I repeatedly made it clear that I was open to respectful conversations and repairing the relationship, but not to being disrespected. Those conversations never happened, and they continue to avoid me.

What has been especially frustrating is that there has never been any acknowledgment that anything wrong happened. His mother's position is that she has done absolutely nothing wrong and therefore has nothing to apologize for. As far as I know, there has never been any genuine attempt to repair the relationship. I admitted my own faults when in frustration after being cropped out of photos and insulted for years reposted a childish mother-in-law type video I saw to my close friends story. It wasn't viewed by many people, but it was still seen and his mother has stated it was hurtful. I have apologized for that post, and yet, she still states she wants "nothing to do with me."

There have been other incidents as well. One sibling made classist comments toward me and another family member defended it as "just a joke" when I stated it wasn't funny. Family members have suggested my partner should meet someone closer to home. His parents have told him that he is "selling himself short" by being in a relationship with me. His mother has also told him that I am negatively affecting her and her husband's health.

There is also a pattern of conversations about me happening when I am not present. Family members regularly express concerns, complaints, or criticisms about me to my partner instead of addressing me directly. Information gets passed through him, which often leaves him stuck in the middle and me feeling like I am defending myself against conversations I wasn't even part of.

The family dynamic in general seems unhealthy. My partner has told me that his mother keeps track of how much money family members spend on gifts and notices when she feels someone has spent less than expected. During a visit, he also discovered there was a family group chat that included everyone except him. He was visibly hurt by that. There have been other situations where family members have criticized him for having different opinions or made him feel like an outsider.

The hardest part is that my partner continues to maintain very close contact with them. He talks to them daily. For a long time he set very few boundaries because he didn't want conflict. More recently, he has started acknowledging that some of their behavior is unhealthy and says he is looking for a therapist.

The problem is that after years of this, I no longer feel comfortable with the level of involvement they have in his/our lives. I don't want my relationship discussed with them. I don't want future children around people who openly dislike me, have told my partner he could do better than me, and have said they would want nothing to do with our future children.

I am not asking him to completely cut them off. However, I have reached a point where I feel like maintaining daily contact and allowing them continued access to our relationship while they actively disrespect me is damaging both to him and to us.

He feels that asking him to distance himself would be unfair because they're still his family. I feel that family relationships should have consequences when people repeatedly mistreat someone's partner and, oftentimes, their own son as well.

Am I overreacting for wanting significantly more distance and stronger boundaries between my partner and his family?

text:
TLDR - Long standing appointments were cancelled with no notice, lies about the reason why and no acknowledgement that it was wrong.

FULL - I have been going to the same salon for almost 10 years. Every 6 weeks. I get services with an esthetician first and then a hair stylist second. I was seeing the hair stylist for about a year before I added the esthetician. It is an indulgence that makes me happy so I allow for it in my budget. Every January, I book a full year’s worth of appointments.

Recently, my hair stylist and esthetician parted ways, each going to a different salon. My hair stylist adjusted my appointment time with her so that I could still see both on the same day and have time to travel between appointments. I did not mention anything to my esthetician because nothing changed for her.

A few days before my appointment, I had a funny feeling so I texted the scheduler at the esthetician’s office. Apparently she had cancelled all of my appointments for the rest of the year because she didn’t think I wanted to travel between appointments. After a little back and forth, she got most of my appointments rebooked.

I just went for my first appointment since. I mentioned to the esthetician that I thought it was odd that I was such a long term customer and didn’t get a call or text before cancelling. She acknowledged that she should have reached out but they had multiple no-shows of people who do appointments similar to mine. I said that I have never so much as been late in 10 years without calling first and that I should have been consulted. She just brushed it off again.

I am really bothered by this. It’s not a small amount of money to me and I don’t like to give my money to people/companies that don’t treat me respectfully. I am always accommodating and understanding and a very low maintenance client to keep happy. What bothers me is that (1) it was done in the first place, (2) she was so dismissive of it being wrong, and (3) I was able to confirm that she lied about the multiple no-shows.

Part of me wants to find a new salon and cancel all of my appointments. Part of me says it’s been 10 years, I can let this slide. Would I be overreacting to find a new salon?

ETA - Thank you all for your replies. I appreciate all of the perspectives. I’d like to make it clear that I am fully aware this isn’t earth shattering, I didn’t think Reddit had a threshold of seriousness to post, I was just curious about how others would perceive this. It sounds like a lot of people think she’s not interested in me as a client so I’ll consider that in my decision.
label: NOR

AIO that my dad (63M) is going overseas on my (19M) birthday? I’m turning 20 on the 5th of July and I’ve been super excited about celebrating because I feel like I’ll actually have (a few) friends to celebrate with this year. I’m also excited because I believe that turning 20 is a big step in the transformation of becoming an adult.
When I turned 16, i spent my birthday alone without family or friends. I was in a very difficult time in my life where i had just been bullied out of my last school, so I was in the summer between that one and my next school I was attending that fall.

I spent it alone because I had no friends and because my dad thought it would be a good idea for him to go to a lake house retreat with his friends and fiancé. It was no secret how suicidal and depressed I was; in fact, they were the ones who even agreed that I switched schools. I was even more depressed about this and voiced it openly about my disappointment and frustration that he wasn’t there for me because of my loneliness and also because I believed that 16 was also a huge turning point in my life in terms of maturity.

This year, I’m going to be 20 and my dad has just informed me that he’s going on a cruise over my birthday. I didn’t even get to find out by him telling me, which made me more upset.
I feel like he’s missing these important milestones in my life and doesn’t care. I have two twin younger brothers turning 19 this year (next week), and my dad and his (now) spouse are in town for them.
AIO? I’m an adult now, which I understand means I should be mature about this, but I also feel like I have a right to be upset. It also upsets me that they’re leaving ON my birthday, so I feel like it’s ridiculous to assume that they “forgot” or anything.

text:
AIO by feeling like my sister in law is judging me and implying that I’m a bad parent/my kids are bad kids? These are texts exchanged between my sister in law (husband’s little sister) while we were on our way over to her house with our kids (twin boys, 12) to spend time her, her husband, and other family (she has a pool etc). I feel like she very heavily criticized my parenting, implied I don’t discipline our kids, and said nasty things about my kids being dirty/rude and making people sick. They’re 12. So yeah no two twelve year old boys are not perfect house guests, shocker. But to say they make her uncomfortable and say they “bum rush” her and imply that it’s their fault the rest of the family besides her and her husband all got pink-eye and (AND IMPLY that the only reason that her and husband didn’t is because they “sanitize” everything my children touch??)

I was in tears in the car and wanted to call off the visit, but when my husband read the texts at a stoplight he told me his sister “wasn’t remotely out of line” to say any of what she said and he told me that he thinks my emotional reaction to this has more to do with the overall hormonal issues I’ve been having due to peri-menopause rather than being due to his sister saying anything bad or mean at all. He also flat out said “she has a point” about our kids being unhygienic and needing to have better manners for their age while I agree that it’s obviously something we need to keep working on, they’re not so bad that she should be saying whatall she said and they’re not worse than other kids their age.

For context: the second time we ever went over to SIL’s house after she bought it, I noticed that she’d put a lock on her main kitchen fridge. It immediately bothered my boys and they came to me to ask about it, so I asked her about it. She told me that her husbands meds are in that fridge so they started using a lock “just in case” I assumed that meant they only locked it when their housekeeper or other workers were over and that she’d take the lock off for us, but she didn’t. She said that she’d put all the food and drinks for us coming over in the mini fridge. It’s a pretty big mini fridge and it’s in the same kitchen as the main fridge and she always stocks it with a ton of fun stuff for my boys and nice snacks for everyone else too. Plus she usually puts out a cooler of adult refreshments too. So while I thought it was weird it was also ok.

But my boys have been consistently complaining about her main fridge being locked during every visit. When they complain to my sil she tells them that she put all the food in the other fridge or she says “what do you want specifically?” And when they say they don’t know what they want because they don’t know what’s in the big fridge and they just to look to see what they can have, she says “then just look in the mini fridge silly!” and fully dismisses them and she’ll do it over and over. This has made them both VERY frustrated. The last time we were over there they were both upset with ME for not getting SIL to open the big fridge.

They really just want to be able SEE what’s in the fridge. In every other family members home they have free range and are allowed to look for themselves and get a snack when they want, and while they can do that in SIL’s mini fridge I think it makes them feel less-than to not be allowed to go into the big fridge. They are VERY curious about whatever forbidden fruit is in there so yes, on the rare occasions when SIL does open the big fridge while we’re over they want to see, I didn’t know they were actually running into her physically when they went to see and that’s obviously not ok and I’ll address that going forward, but I wasn’t going to tell them not to go try to see what’s in the big fridge when she opens it because I just feel like there’s dehumanizing to my children.

I just thought if they could see in the big fridge and she could leave it unlocked then the mystery would be gone and they’d no longer be fixated.

I was not expecting to be told “oh YOUR CHILDREN are the only reason I keep it locked because your kids are gross and rude and kinda fat and you let them run wild so I have to make up for your shitty parenting by locking up my whole house and all MY food”.

And I really wasn’t expecting my husband to think those texts were ok.

We had a fine visit. She was nice as usual, the boys had fun with her and played with her dogs. It was all fine. But I can’t let the things she said in those texts go.

This has been bothering me all day and into the night and I actually can’t sleep.

My husband AND my own dad insist I’m being dramatic and that I need to let it go and that my sil wasn’t actually trying to be mean or imply anything rudely
label: YOR

text:
AIO - Noticing a pattern in the way my girlfriend fights with me, and I don't know how to address it
Me (F 28) and my girlfriend have been together for 8 years, moved in together 2 years ago.

She has ADHD and I have autism. I know that I am sometimes complicated with my needs, I need structure and peace, and she is a bit chaotic and easily distracted. Most of our fights lately have been about house maintenance (I am the one who cleans, she is the one who cooks), social events (she is an extrovert, and I am an introvert), and everyday obligations and plans.

There are days when I can handle it, there are days when I cannot, but that's for a different type of post.

What I've noticed is that every time we fight, I will fight about the actual issue, and she will pull as many ad hominems from her sleeve, which makes me even more furious, overwhelmed, overstimulated and later exhausted.

The most recent fight we've had was about a social event that was full of her friends, colleagues and acquintances. I moderated the event, had a terrible migraine and menstrual cramps during the day and she knew about it, on top of that it was her day off, and I was working from 12:00 h until 20:00 h, and then went to moderate the event.

We went to the after-party, where I expected her to check in with me for at least a few seconds so that I can tell her I'm not feeling well, but as soon as she got into the room, she turned her back and began talking with some of her friends, not even acknowledging that I was there. I also work in the same industry, but she is more successful and famous, so she knows more people than I do. I approached two of our mutual friends and spent the next hour with them.

Every time I tried to check in with my girlfriend, she was fully engaged in the conversation with other people. Mind you, only one person at the table she was at knew about us (homophobic country, not fully publicly out), so I felt like jumping in and telling her "Let's go home" would be weird, given the fact that those people don't know who I am, don't know that we're together, and don't know that we live together.

After an hour the after party ended, but some of the guests wanted to go to an improvised after-after party at a local park. My girlfriend, still in the conversation with other guests, decided that we will go to the after-after party without even asking me if I'd be okay with it. I got mad, of course, and I told her that I was exhausted and wanted to go home, to which she responded that we won't stay long. I went along with it, but was still mad that it didn't even occur to her to ask me. She then asked me why I was mad and I told her that we can talk about it when we get home.

Long story short, we were at the park, she was chatting with those other guests, still ignoring me. I was trying my best to be engaged in conversation with a guy who gave me some very weird, pervy vibes. I eventually came up to her and told her I really need to go home, and she told me to just give her 5 more minutes. Migraine, cramps, temperature shift from a warm room to chilly outside weather right after a rainstorm, and a shot of Jäger combined suddenly made me feel extremely sick, so I just walked off from the spot and hid behind a tree to puke. I collapsed, she ran after me, and we went back home.

I told her that I wish she would've acknowledged my presence at the party because I didn't want to intrude and be pushy. We fought and it went somewhat like this:

Me: You didn't even give me 5 seconds of attention. If you did, I would've told you what was going on.

Her: Yeah, the best solution to this would be to give you all my attention and talk only to you.

Me: Talk to whomever you want, I don't care, just check in with me after a fucked up day I've had. I texted you three times that I was feeling unwell and it didn't even occur to you to ask me if I was okay.

Her: I didn't even see those messages.

Me: You didn't even check your phone? For three hours?

Her: Why would I check my phone if I'm attending an event?

Me: To check what time it is...

Her: I have a watch for that. And you telling me that we'll talk when we get home... I'm not your kid. Talk to me there, at that very moment.

Me: How am I supposed to talk to you about our relationship and our plans in front of people I don't know and they don't know we're even together? Am I supposed to make a scene in order for you to realise that I need you to ask me before making a decision that involves both of us?

Her: You did make a scene! You puked in the park. I have no time nor nerves for this drama. You are controlling, and you want all my attention. Sorry, the attention you got is all the attention I can offer, if you're not okay with that, we shouldn't be together.

Me: I only asked for a few seconds of your time. And all this time I was trying to avoid puking in front of other people.

Her: I'm not planning on babysitting you every time we are at a public event.

.... and it goes on. Every time we fight, she makes it about my character, and always brings breakup into the fight. After it, all I can do is just feel completely and absolutely empty for days. I love her to death, and we don't fight often, but when we do, it's exhausting, I feel like all she wants to do is to hurt me and not actually discuss the situation. And to her, it's weird that I keep repeating the reason of the fight. Like, if I said that all I needed was 5 seconds of her time, she'll be furious because "Why are you always doing this? You're always repeating the same thing in circles", but I am fighting about that thing, not about whether I'm needy or whether she's ignoring me as our character traits. I am fighting about that particular situation.

Am I overreacting? Am I weird for bringing the fight/makeup conversation back to what actually caused it? I am seriously confused. How do I explain that her insults and breakup threats are even more destabilising at that particular moment?
label: NOR

text:

that, and they just weren’t allowing me to attend because they “thought school wasn’t for me.”

fast forward to now, i have a job, have recently opened a savings—on my 18th birthday because my dad refused to help me otherwise—and am attending a nearby university so that i can afford it and pay out of pocket on my own.
i do not have enough to cover even half of my tuition as of now, only enough for housing, so i picked up an interview at a second job serving to help save.
i told my mom, and the second i did, she demanded that i cancel it immediately because there “was no point in me having two jobs.” she explained that it didn’t even make sense to her for me to want two jobs, and even brought up school. when i told her it was to help pay for school, she rolled her eyes, scoffed, and told me to cancel it anyways.
she also told me that she wouldn’t help with transportation at all, and i can’t afford to do uber right now because i have been paying for my freshman things.

i just feel like a child here. nothing i say or do is respected, nor listened to or cared about. they’ve assigned summer reading for me, gotten upset about my preference to work rather than go on vacation, etc.
there’s even a point where they won’t let me use their car anymore because i don’t want to change my license address to my grandmother’s—who lives in a different state. they’re wanting me to do it for cheaper insurance.
i’m even okay with getting a car of my own, but the only way they’re willing to let me save for it is if THEY give half of the money to me as well—which i have declined because i’m convinced that if i did something they didn’t like, they’d take it from me.

basically, am i overreacting for feeling like there isn’t room for me to grow up in my home? and if possible—how can i declare the boundaries that i want?
