text:
AIO? My (20F) therapist took on my abusive ex (31M) as an individual client.
Hey everyone, I really need to vent and honestly get some perspective on a massive ethical mess that just happened with my therapist. This is unfortunately a very true story that I am living in currently.
 
To give some context, I (20F) have been seeing my therapist (LMFT, 28M) for a few months. I started seeing him after a domestic abuse court case began involving a mutually abusive and toxic, highly volatile relationship with my ex-boyfriend. This court case includes protective orders.
 
At the very beginning, as my court case began, I was trauma-bonded with my ex and desperately wanted to make the relationship work. I wanted us to try couple’s therapy, which I told my therapist. My therapist offered to take us on as a couple, despite acknowledging that it is not typical to have your individual therapist be your couple’s therapist. I expressed a bit of concern and anxiety with this arrangement as I did not want my then-boyfriend to mess with my individual therapy. But my therapist assured me I would always be the priority client and that it would not get in the way. My ex and I did exactly two couple’s therapy sessions with this therapist before I ended up cutting things off with my ex. An important note is that my ex-boyfriend already had his own individual therapist. This was supposed to be couple’s therapy for us both. That and nothing more.
 
After the breakup, I stayed with the therapist for individual sessions, as was my original intention with therapy. Because of the court case, my therapy sessions are tracked and sent directly to my DVCC case advocate. It started off voluntary, but eventually it became a court-mandated part of my case.
 
When my ex and I broke up and I decided I no longer wanted to do couple’s therapy, my therapist continued to see my ex-boyfriend without me present. I found this very odd as my therapist had told me he would not take him on individually. Additionally, my ex already has his own therapist. My therapist claimed that seeing my ex was an extension of our couples therapy and that it was “extra support in regard to the couple’s therapy”, or something along those lines. The only justification I could give to my therapist for doing this is that I was not 100% sure about this breakup – as a young and vulnerable client with trauma and in a very volatile relationship -- of course it was hard to leave my relationship and of course my heart was still attached and questioning. To reiterate, the only justification I could give my therapist is that “Well.. maybe me and (ex-boyfriend) would have worked it out..”
But as time went on, I began to get more uncomfortable with the fact that my ex-boyfriend continued and continued to see my therapist individually for weeks. I became more certain of our breakup. I became more uncomfortable with this extremely odd conflict-of-interest arrangement. He was seeing my ex-boyfriend, but I wasn’t allowed to know any details due to “confidentiality”. My therapist was so involved in my affairs, yet I could not know any details. Imagine the anxiety and loss of trust this caused between my therapist and I. I felt sick thinking about all the unsavory things my ex must have been feeding my therapist and how this could affect how my therapist sees me and how he interprets everything I say. I began to feel that my ex-boyfriend was controlling me and gaining power over me by seeing my therapist when he already has his own.
 
In fact, this ex-boyfriend continues to try to contact me despite being blocked in every single way possible and completely ex-communicated. Despite his phone number being blocked and a protective order, he consistently leaves me short voicemails and leaves me little gifts/treats in my car whilst I have no contact with him. He breadcrumbs me and tries to maintain control over me and keep himself in my thoughts without ever apologizing or properly taking accountability. I find it vile. It should be worth nothing that I told my therapist about these things. For concerned Redditors, I would rather not involve police or make my court case any more complicated at this point in time. The court case has been ongoing for months and I anticipate its conclusion next month. But that is not the point of this. I am not asking for relationship advice, I am asking for opinions on this situation with my therapist. I have already completely cut my ex off. Please allow me to continue.
 
Anyways, I explicitly expressed my concerns to my therapist about him continuing to see my ex. I have it all on paper trail. I was terrified of my ex trying to worm his way into my safe spaces. My therapist told me that because couples therapy was not “unequivocally off the table”, he continued to see my ex. My therapist and I said we would discuss this more in therapy. Because of the trauma-bonded nature of this relationship, it was so hard for me to be able to say, “It is over for good.” But that doesn’t mean that my therapist needed to continue to see my ex-boyfriend. I am an extremely vulnerable young client. This should have been considered and handled with care.
During this time, I submitted my verification of treatment to my DVCC court case advocate as I needed to show the court I was doing therapy. My advocate then called me concerned because she saw that my therapist was also seeing my ex who is also involved in the court case! This made me realize just how much more concerning this is. I was getting increasingly uncomfortable and stressed with this situation that was out of my control. I never wanted my ex to see my therapist individually, and this was something behind the smoke and mirrors of “confidentiality”, so I never knew for sure what was going on until I was slapped right in the face with my DVCC advocate calling me out on it and exposing the truth once she got my ex boyfriend’s letter from my therapist.
 
Well, cut to a few weeks ago. My therapist suddenly started canceling on me at the last minute. One week he is sick and tells me he will reschedule me later that week but never does, two weeks he is on vacation, and three weeks he has to reschedule. I am getting very fed up at this point. One, this could be messing with my court compliance. Two, this is just disrespectful and inconsiderate. Three, I am already pissed about the situation with my ex.
 
So, I finally called him out very directly. I sent my therapist a long letter on paper trail detailing everything I have said here in essence. How it is so messed up that he allowed my abusive ex to commandeer my safe space when he already had his own therapist and his own resources. How he went against his own word. How this is ethically wrong, it is a conflict of interest, and how it is not okay. I expressed how upset I was that I may have to lose my safe space and lose all my progress and rapport I built because he allowed my ex to do this. I even said that I may have to defend myself by using the proper channels to report this behavior if I end up having to lose my care because of my ex. I basically threatened him, but I feel justified in this to my core.
 
I was completely devastated and disgusted with my therapist. I do not even know how I can trust him anymore. I do not know what he knows about me from my ex. I do not know what he thinks about me or the things I say as a result. I do not believe my therapist had my back, He should have terminated their relationship the very first time I expressed concerns, but I do not believe he did.
 
Now I am no saint, I was also an abusive piece of shit in this horrible excuse of a relationship. But I want to mention that my ex has a history of physical abuse (he has choked me, slammed me into walls and onto the floor, leaving physical scars) and a pattern of violating my privacy, feeding people information about me, and turning everyone in his life against me to isolate me. I told my therapist all these things. My therapist completely threw my safety out the window and gave my abuser a direct platform to sit on his couch, twist the narrative, and destroy my only safe space. I was left spiraling, wondering if my own therapist secretly hated me or was judging me based on whatever garbage my ex was feeding him.
 
Anyways, when I sent my therapist this message, he went from taking an hour to respond after telling me he must cancel/reschedule the THIRD WEEK IN A ROW, to replying within 12 minutes when I called him out on this BULLSHIT. He completely changed his tune. He said some corporate, thinly veiled "I can neither confirm nor deny attendance due to HIPAA" garbage, but then immediately followed it up by saying he has "already discontinued the situation" to "prioritize my clinical progress."
 
I know he didn't do it for my sake. He did it because I caught him, called him out, threatened his license, named a court advocate, and he realized he was facing a malpractice paper trail that could destroy his career. Either that or my ex’s lawyers probably also told him he looked like a stalker for stealing his ex's therapist and told him to pull out.
 
Either way, I know my therapist did not do this to protect me. I know he did not have my back in this situation. I feel so wronged. My ex never protected me, and then my therapist failed to protect me, too. It is so fucking sad that this happens to someone who is already struggling so much.
 
The worst part is I have a session tomorrow to address this, and I am terrified he is just going to try to drop me as a client in a few weeks out of retaliation because of me calling him out, basically threatening him, and standing up to him. This is a way amplified threat, since my therapist currently sees me for free. What incentive does he have to keep me? I am a liability. He gains nothing. (He offered to see me for free as I am facing financial issues, contrary to the depiction of my therapist I have given. This is a bit confusing. Part of me sees my therapist as kind and generous and a good person, and another part of me feels totally wronged and disrespected on such a deep level.  How confusing that my therapist would do something so nice for me then something so detrimentally negligent and hurtful?) My point is, it would be so easy for him to just say, "Oops! No more free. I have to charge full price now," knowing I can't afford it, just to legally force me out for standing up for myself.
 
Am I right to be this angry? Can I report this?  Could he lose his license for this or be reprimanded? Has anyone else ever dealt with a therapist crossing lines like this? How do I even survive a confrontation session with someone who holds all the cards over my court case? I need to continue therapy, or I will be in trouble with the court. So terminating is not something I can do at the drop of a hat, or I will have a lapse in mandated treatment.
 
I really do not want to lose all my progress and be forced to find a new therapist. I truly do not. I valued the relationship that my therapist and I had. But realistically, the trust is broken here and it seems like that may be an inevitability. Maybe my therapist will terminate tomorrow.
 
If you read all of this, you are amazing. Thank you.

text:
AIO for hating an elderly woman over some eggs?
My BF (M25) and I (F38) have been together for 5 years. When I first met his grandparents, they had a chicken coop, some goats, and a vegetable garden. However, after about a year, they had to get rid of everything (except for one rooster) due to mobility issues.

Since then, my boyfriend and I have taken over the care of the entire property: the land, the old coop, the stable, and the old greenhouse. Everything was in terrible shape: old, filthy, infested with rats, and cluttered with junk (scrap metal, old machinery parts, car pieces, rusty water heaters, and boards with rusty nails sticking out). We’ve been slowly cleaning it all up, though it's still a work in progress.

On top of that, I always help his grandparents whenever they need anything. I’ve driven them to the hospital at 3 AM, I take them to the bank, buy their groceries and medication, and I even cut their nails. We’ve always had a really good relationship.

A few months ago, we ended up getting chickens. It wasn't planned, but it was a family commitment we had to step up for. Even though I’m a city girl at heart, I’ve fallen completely in love with country life. I do most of the farm work because I genuinely love it. I buy their feed, got them new waterers, and rewired the entire enclosure to keep predators out. I'm learning how to build nesting boxes, and I collect fresh grass for them daily. I even started breeding mealworms for them and planted specific greens to supplement their diet. Honestly, taking care of my small garden and the coop makes me feel like I'm in heaven.

However, his grandmother is completely against us having chickens. She claims the roosters disturb the neighbors. But we barely have any neighbors—we live in a rural zone. In fact, the land is officially zoned as rural by the local town hall. This area used to be full of animals and crops, but as the older generation aged, they got rid of everything, and the younger generation had no interest in keeping it going. We are literally the last ones left keeping livestock. She also thinks coops are inherently loud and messy because when they ran it, they kept all the chickens and roosters cooped up together, leading to inbreeding and disease. But I only keep one rooster, and he is not loud at all.

Eventually, some of the chickens started laying and incubating eggs. I was so incredibly happy to experience this. I checked on them, marked the eggs, wrote down the expected dates, and prepared a safe space for the chicks and their mothers.

But right around the hatching date, all the eggs vanished. I was devastated. We blamed rats, thieves, a jealous neighbor... We installed security cameras, set rat traps, and changed all the locks on the gates.

Soon after, my chickens started incubating a second clutch of eggs. And the exact same thing happened: the eggs disappeared. We checked the security camera footage, and to my absolute horror, the culprit was his grandmother.

I am beyond furious. Right now, my boyfriend and I are spending our free time completely rewiring her house and installing a new bathroom adapted to her mobility needs.

But after seeing the footage, I just walked away, blind with rage. I was so close to having baby chicks. I feel like my boyfriend bought me a ticket to Disneyland, and his grandmother stole it and tore it to pieces right in front of me.

I need someone to tell me that I'm overreacting and why, because as of right now, my relationship with her is changed forever.

Edit1:

-I don't own the property. I stay 4 days a week to help his grandparents with basic stuff like meals, using the bathroom, etc... his grandfather uses a wheelchair and the house is not adapted.

Edit2:

-If the age gap is such a concern for some of you, let me clarify a few things to clear up any assumptions that I’m taking advantage of him:

First, he was the one who pursued me. We met while studying electricity and electronics. I was the only girl in class who shared his passion for disassembling old washing machines.

Second, I am asexual and I don't want children.

Lastly, if anyone thinks he’s being trapped or used, let me lay out my reality: I have multiple sclerosis, I am currently unemployed, and I’m slightly overweight. My only real social interactions are on the internet and talking to my chickens. My parents are well-off enough that they’ve told me I don't need to work due to my illness (though I am actively looking for a job and making some money selling my crafts).

I am hardly a 'trophy girlfriend' or a gold digger. I don't even know what’s in his bank account, and we have an open agreement where he is free to have sex with other women. He is with me because he wants to be, and he is free to leave whenever he pleases.

UPDATE: We found a solution (and a lot of clarity)

It’s only been two days since my original post, but so much has happened. I read my post and your comments to my boyfriend (he doesn't speak English), and we spent hours talking through everything. With over 200 comments, we had a lot to process. Here is how things stand now:

Addressing the "Legal" and "Property" comments: To those saying "it’s not your property" or "the grandma could call the cops on you" honestly, that just sounds like a very American perspective. In Europe, people don’t immediately threaten lawsuits, lawyers, or police over family dynamics. My boyfriend found those comments baffling, and we've decided to ignore them. The house he lives in belongs to his grandmother, but he has spent years doing heavy maintenance on it for free because it’s understood to be family property. She is not going to call the police on us.

Addressing the attacks on our relationship (Age gap / "Creep" accusations): When I told my boyfriend that some people were practically accusing me of being a predator because of our age gap, he literally burst out laughing. He started running around the yard pinching my butt and yelling, "Help, I’m being taken advantage of!"

Then he reminded me of the first time he invited me over. We wanted to build a Jacob's Ladder, but since it was too dangerous to do indoors, we went out to the empty garden. We used an old microwave transformer, and after a few failed attempts, we actually got it to work! We brought it to class, and our electronics professor loved it so much he kept the model to show future students. My boyfriend told me that was the exact moment he knew he wanted to be with me, regardless of my health or circumstances. Five years later, he doesn't regret a single thing. So, we are completely ignoring those weird comments.

Neither my boyfriend nor I are confrontational people. We usually just turn the other cheek. When I first saw the camera footage, I was too furious to speak, so I just went home. I tried to act polite to his grandmother on my way out, but I'm sure my anger was visible.

Interestingly, for those who thought the grandmother hates me: she actually assumed I was leaving because my boyfriend had done something wrong. She started apologizing for him, calling him an idiot, and saying, "Thank God you're here to knock some common sense into his thick skull." Now that I have a cold head, I don’t think she acts out of malice or hatred, she is just incredibly unempathetic and genuinely doesn’t understand the emotional damage she caused.

When I got home, I was incredibly dismissive and shut down until I could process it. My boyfriend came over right after work. He brought me a box of Sylvanian Families (I think they are called Calico Critters in America), and we just sat, played, and talked.

When I read him the post, he immediately admitted that he had been letting too much responsibility fall on my shoulders without realizing it. On the flip side, to those who said I was taking advantage of him, things got worse for you. We came to an agreement: from now on, he is covering everything. Every single expense for the garden, the chickens, or when we go out to dinner or the movies, he is taking care of me. He even gave me access to his Amazon and AliExpress accounts, not so I can buy things directly, but so I can drop whatever I need into his cart.

Regarding the "overstepping" comments: IMO you guys were right, but my boyfriend didn't think so, and took charge of this completely. He took the keys to the coop away from his grandmother. She no longer has access to it, nor can she send anyone else in. Since she keeps complaining about the noise, we've decided to rehome the roosters. However, since almost nobody raises animals around here anymore, they will likely end up being culled. The grandmother still isn't happy, I don't know if she just doesn't believe us or if it's just old age.

My boyfriend told me that under no circumstances will he tolerate anyone taking my chickens or my garden away. He said he has never seen me happier, that he loves building projects with me, and that he wants us to get married and live here permanently. And I believe him.

He has built a private bathroom just for me right next to the coop (with a portable camping potty and a water tank he fills by hand). In the house, he is building me a PC so we can work on software projects and I can game (it’s just an i3 for now, but he upgrades it piece by piece). He buys all the filament for my 3D printer (I design custom technical parts in Blender, but also miniatures for my dolls), and he gave me my own side of the workshop.

As for his grandparents, I will still help, but I am limiting myself strictly to what is necessary. He completely understands that I can’t look at his grandmother the same way anymore. If they need extra help, they will have to rely more on the state-funded social worker who comes a couple of days a week.

I don't know how things will go long-term, but a massive weight has been lifted off my chest. I can't put into words the love and affection I feel for those chickens, and I don't care if that sounds silly or overly sentimental.

Thank you all for your input. It really helped us see things clearly.

text:
AIO for locking the door to my grandparents’ part of our house
My (F23) grandma suffers from Alzheimer’s. She used to live alone with my grandfather, but it got so bad that their house is now literally RUINED. He can’t cook, he can’t clean, he can’t take care of himself nor her. So following my mother’s invitation — they moved in, and now I’ve become an almost full time caretaker of my elderly pets and them. I didn’t choose that, I’m only here because one of the very few things she actually remembers is that she hates animals, so she abuses them on autopilot. Only a few days ago she tried to kick my cat, but thankfully it was on the other side of our glass door.

They have their own semi-apartment with their own kitchen, their own bathroom, their own living room, bedroom and everything. Most of the day they stay there.

And I work online. Sometimes my meetings take me 20 minutes, sometimes they take me 1hr, sometimes they take me 2hrs, and during these times I rely on my grandfather. My mom works from 9am until 9pm, so I can’t ask her for help.

Except that the SECOND I leave my living room, she leaves their own living room and tries to throw all my animals outside. Nothing works, obviously, because she’s unable to learn at this stage of her disorder. Asking, begging, yelling, nothing. My grandfather doesn’t care. He just lets her do whatever she wants. Sometimes AFTER I CALL FOR HIM he yells at her and she gets scared enough to stop. It’s basically nuts. Most of the time I have to get away from my work to stop her from abusing my pets anyway.

So today was the day I decided to close the door connecting our living room to their entire part of the house. I woke up at 7:30 after I’d spent the entire night preparing pizza and sugar free cake for them (my grandma’s diabetic). I had a meeting from 8:00 until 8:20, so I was gone for literally less than half an hour. I got back to the kitchen and EVERYTHING was smeared in her shit. Everything. She touched EVERY piece of food I’d prepared. The oven, too. Of course I threw everything away. Her husband? Not even in the same room with her. I thought I’d cry.

So I stood up and I told him that I’d need to start locking that door whenever I can’t be there with her, and I gave him the only key in case he needed something… and now he won’t speak to me anymore. Bro’s so mad he walks away whenever I try to sit with them for some time together. Am I crazy? Was it too much?? He specifically said he DOESN’T want to give her away to a nursing home 🥲 ever.

So, AIO? AITA? I feel like I’m losing my marbles, and my mom is of no help either. She’s on vacation now, so I’m alone with all… this.

EDIT: I forgot to mention that I had to take a 1 year long break from my uni because otherwise they’d be here alone with my animals for long periods of time and I kind of resent everybody from my family for that. So I’m not super nice to them, I just make food and clean shit.

UPDATE:
Grandpa is no longer mad at me. I bought him a typewriter a couple of days ago and it arrived today. Excitement took over.

The key works. Grandma can’t enter the living room at all anymore. Strictly forbidden. I just spend a lot of time in their part of the house with them. After my mother comes back, the plan is to register my grandparents as residents of the city we live in and get a nurse. So next week. Than you all very much for your insights and suggestions ❤️And also for being nice to me, because sometimes I feel like the bad guy here, no matter what I do.

text:
AIO For Wanting to Divorce After No Support From Job Loss
Throwaway account for obvious reasons.

I (43F) and my spouse (45M) have been together for 20 years this August. He is bad at being helpful during crisis or significant negative events. We defer drastically in this aspect as I’m great in those situations, am often the first call for a number of folx, and his own mom will call me first with emergencies before him or either of his two sisters (her daughters.) I have repeatedly told him that I really need a spouse who will be there for me when bad stuff happens.

This came to a head last week. I was unexpectedly terminated from my job and lost benefits the day after (had they terminated me two days later I would have had benefits until the end of the month.) This left me scrambling, calling the company HR to figure out next steps after my manager called to tell me. I worked immediately on appealing the decision (still in process), trying to figure out healthcare resources as a stopgap while I am uninsured, calling law firms since this was partially not accommodating for a documented ADA disability (inattentive ADHD), and so forth.

I asked my spouse to see about adding me to their health insurance in the meantime until I got another job or my appeal got approved and I got reinstated (I already know Cobra would be cost prohibitive because the company has very competitive benefits package.) I asked him everyday last week about it, and today (8 days later) he says he finally talked to someone but turns out they’re not the expect on it but they believed this was a qualifying event so I should be able to be added on without waiting for open enrollment - but would need to ask the expert tomorrow to be for sure. He said that in the meantime it was more important that I didn’t have a lapse in insurance since that would be a tax penalty next year … I was shocked because I told him I already have a gap and have been uninsured for 8 days now. He then tells me he didn’t realize and didn’t understand when I told him. Of note I had told him repeatedly last week how it was messed up that a difference of two days meant I was without benefits very suddenly, told him multiple times in front of multiple different audiences (I told his family over the weekend.) This is what sets off the argument.

The other components to this is my stark realization that he did not ask me once during the week how I was doing, nor asked me if he could help with anything. I have done at least 3 dozen phone calls and emails this past week trying to sort out stuff. In fact he added to my plate this week instead of taking stuff off of it. I asked him on Thursday if he would accompany me to the garden (local botanical garden that helps with my mental health.) He said no, that he had work to do that was easier to do at home … then proceeded to work on something that did not have a deadline and was optional for him to work on. I asked a few times on Friday if he would come to the garden, he declined again. I finally asked, “hey, could you please come to the garden with me to help support me.” He paused, did a very loud exasperated sigh and said “Since you asked, I guess so” in a ‘resigned to his fate’ type of tone.

Anyways fast forward to today. And in this personal life-altering situation (this was a career position, I have been with that company for over a decade) I realized that not only did he never check in on me nor offer help, the two things I did ask help with he either did begrudgingly or punted the task for over a week.

This is not the first time when I’ve had some serious situation happen and he failed spectacularly in supporting me. I got so used to it that it was the reason why with this situation I just immediately acted on my own behalf and didn’t think to even ask for his help since I know he’s not a good person to ask. I asked what the heck he was thinking with last week and he talked about how he was focused on how he could keep things stable and how to keep himself from panicking … so selfishly only thinking about how my firing affects him and never once asked me how I was feeling about it. In stark contrast I’ve had family, friends, and former coworkers reach out to ask how I’m doing and if there is anything they can help with.

So I am now moving towards divorce (state law requires legal separation for X months before proceeding with divorce.) He says he knows he messed up badly, even says now that he doesn’t see any other way around this, he simply “did not support me in the way you deserve” (his words.) We don’t have children, so this isn’t a stopping point to try and work this out.

TL:DR AIO for wanting to proceed with divorce after I was fired and my spouse did not ask how I was doing, did not offer to help, failed to help with the one request I had for him, and he did one other request but did so begrudgingly (request he did was just accompanying me for moral support while I went to my mental health space location.)

text:
AIO? i want to leave my job asap
I am 20F, I work full time as an admin assistant and I am struggling heavily at my job.

I started my job in November and I absolutely loved it. I loved the environment, work-life balance etc. but lately things have gone very south VERY FAST. It is a 5 person office, and Boss + Manager are very close knit.

ISSUE 1: My boss, owner of the company, has literally never said a word to me. He doesn't talk to any of the admin staff and instead has our manager relay messages/grievances for him instead of talking to us himself. He makes many remarks walking past us suggesting we are dirty and lazy.

" I wonder what their houses look like, " as we didn't clean his dishes.

" I've never seen staff like this before, they go on their lunch and scurry off in their little corners. " as we don't sit at our desks during our unpaid lunches. He said this one walking past me alone, loud enough so I'd hear.

One of the admins who's been there longer than me has told me about a time he's got in her face screaming and swearing at her as she made a mistake on the schedule. He did not attempt a civil conversation and instead belittled her whilst our manager was on holiday. He had our manager apologies to her on his behalf.

He is constantly angry in the office and it creates an awful atmosphere, and he yells a lot. I don't like playing this card but I am a young woman and I find him very intimidating. I walk on eggshells around him and find myself nervous to even go to the bathroom/water cooler in case he gets angry.

ISSUE 2: This happened today. I walked into work and I was handed a list of my job responsibilities, and it also outlined my co-workers responsibilities. I have lost 90% of my old responsibilities and now only have the 'bad' or 'boring' jobs to do, which only take 2 hours maximum of my time to complete. My coworkers have kept all their original tasks AND new ones, where they'll receive a pay rise. I do not understand why I have been singled out, I have never given anyone a reason to question my work ethic. I am early to work everyday, i DO sit at my desk on a lot of my lunches to help when it's busy.

I also have been surprised with a job title change which was not stated in my contract nor have I been offered a new one. I came into this job as an admin assistant and today I was referred to as the junior admin assistant which was never stated in my contract or job offer. I am extremely confused, this is my first 'real' job and I have no idea if this is normal or not.

I am deeply hurt by all of this, I give this job my all and I feel as though it's been thrown in my face. I asked my manager why it has changed and she told me, ' you do too much, your name is on all of the jobs ' (we allocate work to engineers), I DON'T KNOW WHAT THIS MEANS. Surely that's a good thing? I am so confused.

I feel like I am being slowly phased out, or their at least trying to get me to go. We don't need 3 admins and I have the least experience, and I am the youngest. I don't know how else to understand what's happened.

ISSUE 3: In regards to my bosses remark of, " I've never seen staff like this before, they go on their lunch and scurry off in their little corners. " I did bring this up to my manager also, and she got quite angry because we asked? Manager and Boss are very, very close- grew up together working in the company, so I am unsure if we offended her as they're like family but I don't think his remarks are appropriate at all. I asked, ' Boss said X, I didn't understand how to take that other than a remark on how we spend our personal lunches, ' and it turned very sour, and she walked away after giving a very random, vague answer about a chip on the floor. Manager has always been very 'against-drama' but seems to facilitate Boss making nasty remarks about the employees he's never even bothered to get to know. I heard him ask what my name was after a month of working there.

In all honestly, I am ready to go. I am done with the environment and I think them demoting me quietly was the ultimate slap in the face and I can't see myself bouncing back from that. I find myself dreading going into office the last few weeks and now I likely will be pulling a sicky for a few weeks until I find somewhere else. Very immature of me but I always treat others how they've treated me. I don't want advice on how to fix the workplace as I am mentally checked out- I just want to know if this is normal and something I should just expect forever.

There's no form of HR department, I can only go to my manager- which I tried- but even that is not a viable option for me by her reaction when I asked about my Boss' remark. I just feel lost, a bit stuck and deeply hurt how I've been cast aside.

text:

text:
AIO over my husband doubting everything I say
My husband (26) debates against everything that I (23F) say, and he insists that it's normal.

I say that I feel mentally drained because it takes a lot of energy to have to defend every decision I make, and everything that I say. Most of the time as of late he acts like he thinks I'm significantly intellectually inferior to him and it is both hurtful and angering.

We could be talking about literally anything. He could ask me for my opinion on what he should do in xyz situation, I'll give him my input and then he'll just debate against it anyway leaving me wondering why he asked for it in the first place if he doesn't trust my judgment.

I used to clean houses for a living and I did it for six years. I passively said something about how bleach cannot be mixed with alkaline floor cleaners and instead of recognizing that I have over half a decade's worth of cleaning experience he argued with me about how he's done it before and he's still alive and that means I'm wrong, and after an HOUR, he finally googled it, tried to mince the words while reading the google AI overview to make it sound like he was right and then gave up when I looked it up myself.

He asked me if he should block his toxic ex friend's friends because they were viewing all his socials, so I said I personally would, because I don't see any reason to keep ties between myself and someone in my past who was left there for a solid reason. he kept pushing back against what I was saying so I eventually just asked him why he asked for my input if he already made up his mind. He said "Woah, chill out you don't need to be like that."

I asked him what he meant and he said I was giving him attitude unprovoked. I said I thought he was honestly trying to ask for my input and I was annoyed because instead he just threw me into a debate I wasn't in the mood for. He said that he wasn't debating with me, that he was just offering his own opposing perspective. I told him that was literally the same thing, and he said "No it isn't, it's a normal conversation."

I said it wasn't normal, and I asked him if he constantly disagrees with other people he talks to as well. He said "Constantly?!" as if he were baffled by my wording when this is an every day occurrence. I said yes and that he disagrees with pretty much every opinion I have. I've seen him put more faith in an AI summary or a random instagram reel than he has in anything I have to say. (mind you most of the time it will be a literal fucking advertisement from a page promoting some stupid obscure bs like amphibian toe meat while insisting that every other animal is cancerous to consume).

Then he said "Well maybe it's because most of the things you say are disagreeable." I just froze for a moment, then said "Do you think I'm stupid?" He scoffed at me and said "God you are so fucking ridiculous sometimes." I asked how I'm being ridiculous when he literally just said that most of what I have to say is wrong in his eyes. He then said that I was being ridiculous because I was expecting him to just blindly agree with everything I say, and I said "No, I expect the man who married me to not blindly DISAGREE with everything that I say. It's exhausting having to sit here and debate with you every time I speak to you."

He said "So wheres the balance then? You don't want me to agree with everything you say but you also don't want me to disagree with everything you say. Am I supposed to just nod and smile at you whenever you talk? Why is the woman I married making me walk on eggshells?"

I don't want him to feel like he's walking in eggshells, so I felt really bad and I apologized. But the more I think about it the more I wonder how I made him feel that way when all I wanted was to not feel doubted every time I spoke. Am I missing something here??

text:
When our cat attacks me, my partner feels attacked, AIO?
Context:

My (37f) partner (37m) and I have been together for almost 10 years. We have 2 young children, share a home, and a black cat called Luna (2f).

We had 2 dogs and a siamese that we brought into our blended family, but they have all since passed of old age- mentioned for understanding that we have successfully been pet owners together.

Luna came to us very young. She was found alone in a golf course behind my BIL's home. We took her to the vet, and she became a member of our family.

We had a newborn at the time, so Luna would sit on my shoulder while I breastfed our daughter, she would play with the baby's toys during tummy time, she had a full on playground in our eldest daughter's bedroom, and she played well with our other pets (previously mentioned).

As Luna got older, she began attacking me and being extremely rough with me at times.

She would attack my back and arms when changing the baby's diaper, jump and hang from my back when I was at a sink/countertop, bite at my ankles when I would walk down the hallway, etc.

She didn't do this to anyone else in the house. She's always been extremely loving and playful with my partner. She meows and talks to him, lays in his lap, rubs on him, etc.

She plays well with our kids and is extremely gentle with them.

Over time, I have tried many different things. I play with her using different toys and laser lights, I feed her special wet food, and I give her treats. I pet her when she allows me to, and I don't push myself on her when she's not in the mood.

For the last year, her behaviors have changed. She doesn't do the other things I've mentioned before. Now, she attacks at more specific times.

When it's bedtime, if I crack my partner's back, or I pick her up for any reason.

If I pick her up, she is calm until I put her down, then she attacks me.

If I massage my partner's back or crack his back, she attacks me within the hour.

When it's bedtime -EVERY NIGHT, she hunts me down the hall and tries to bite my feet/ankles while I'm getting into bed. If I jump in quickly so she can't bite my feet, she jumps into bed, hisses at me, then bites the crap out of my arm. It hurts more because she basically punches my arm with her face, bites, then pulls. She draws blood most of the time. If my partner and I argue, the cat is biting my ankles.

I never hit, yell, or attack back. I have a spray bottle that I hold up, which makes her back off- I've only had to spray her 2x because just seeing it makes her stop. My partner can also get her to stop by just telling her to do so.

Here's the frustrating part....

My partner always gets extremely upset. Not for me but for Luna. He hates that our cat does this because he would never get rid of her - he's sad that he's in this position to think about it.

He has snapped at me for yelping in pain because I scared Luna. He went to her and made sure she was OK while my arm was literally bleeding - that made me cry, which started a fight. I have never complained about her or said that I don't love her - because I do. I never ask to get rid of her.

Tonight, I was setting my laptop up in bed. Luna jumped up, hissed, and then bit my wrist. My partner was saying goodnight to our eldest daughter across the hall, so he heard her hiss and me say, "Ouch, Luna. Why?"

While I was typing, Luna stepped onto my laptop, hissed again, then bit my arm to draw blood. I yelped and said, "Please stop Looney (my nickname for her)."

My partner barged into the room, so Luna walked away. He went into the bathroom stomping and seemingly pissed. He wouldn't look at me. I asked him if he was upset with me.

He said, "Give me a break! My cat loves me and then bites you like that. Imagine how I feel right now!?! I'm not trying to make this about me, but..."

I said ok, and he stormed out of the room. Now he's in his band room with Luna.

So, reddit, AIO? Should I be more sensitive to my partner's situation? What more can I try to do to mend my relationship with Luna?
label: NOR

text:
AIO to my best friend making me feel guilty for being in a relationship?
I (21F) feel like my best friend (20F), who I live with, is constantly judging me for being in a relationship, and I don’t know if I’m overreacting or if this is actually weird.

For some context, I'm a full time student studying human biology, working a part-time job as a waitress. I’ve also been dating my boyfriend for around 2 years.

Now my best friend is very different from me, she’s pre-med, straight A student, works multiple jobs, does research, and is just super career-focused. I genuinely respect that a lot and admire this about her, but she constantly makes comments that feel really "guilty" about my relationship.

For example, she’ll say things like:
“I don’t understand why you’d want to be in a relationship before you have your life figured out.”
“I wouldn’t want to be with someone right now because you never know, you guys could break up and you just wasted that time.”
“You should be spending your time doing something more productive.” “Don’t you want more for yourself than that?”

She also makes comments about my boyfriend that are just out of line. She’s called him ugly, says he's fat, said he doesn’t deserve certain opportunities when it comes to job relevancy, and constantly questions what he’s doing with his "career" or internships. At one point she even asked me to take a picture of his resume so she could make fun of it, which really bothered me? Also because I've just never heard that before in my life?

On top of that, she comments on how I spend my time. When I come home from his place, she’ll ask if I did anything “productive,” and if I say I just hung out with him, she’ll respond with things like “that’s it?” or “so you didn’t do anything productive?”

She also keeps tabs on my location and seems to get annoyed if I’m at his place “too long,” (despite us having no plans) and I’ve found out she’s told our mutual friends that all I care about is my boyfriend.

She also says we’re probably going to break up when he graduates (he’s a year ahead of me), even though nothing about our relationship suggests that.

What’s really starting to get to me is that her comments are getting in my head. Sometimes when I’m with my boyfriend, even when I’m genuinely happy just watching shows with him, I catch myself feeling guilty, like I should be doing something “more productive,” because I hear her voice in the back of my mind.

I don’t judge her at all for focusing on school and her career, in fact it's amazing and I know she'll be successful, but it feels like she’s judging me for choosing a different balance.

I’m happy in my relationship and don’t feel like it’s holding me back, but she makes me question that sometimes; especially since we will be graduating next year and I don't exactly have anything biology related on my resume, nor an internship lined up. So sometimes I feel like she's right and maybe my priorities are wrong even though they make me happy.

Am I overreacting for feeling disrespected by this and spiraling or is this not normal behavior? Or is this just a wake-up call.
label: NOR

text:
AIO- feel neglected in my relationship and I’m trying not to
Talk me off the ledge of wanting to blow it up bc that is what I’m used to doing. My (36F) boyfriend (40M) of almost 2 years moved in with me and my 12 & 14 year old 2 months ago.

He works Tuesday-Saturday and every Sunday he goes skateboarding with friends. He’s gone from 6/7am to 9/10pm on Sundays. I work Monday to Friday. Which leaves us no days off together.

I knew this about him, and I chose to move forward with our relationship. I thought that having him around more during the week would help me feel differently about this lack of QT. I also naively thought once we lived together HE would choose to skip out once in awhile knowing how I feel but he hasn’t, and now I’m at the point of having anxiety Saturday nights knowing he’s about to be up(waking me up) at the ass crack of dawn to ditch me all day lol. Makes me feel ridiculous for being so bothered by it.

He will skip Sundays if we have something important planned. But just spending the day at home together and getting stuff done etc, isn’t enough to forgo it.

I want to support what makes him happy, skating is everything to him, but EVERY Sunday, ALL damn day?

Him and his friends will drive HOURS away, planning these outings. If I’m totally honest.. I’m a little hurt that he doesn’t put that kind of effort into planning anything for us 🥲

He prioritizes me and my kids in other ways but this just feels excessive (and a bit juvenile at our age of responsibilities but I’ll never tell him that).

I’ve talked to him about how I feel but I don’t want to make him feel guilty about something that makes him so happy nor resent me for trying to “take away” what he loves…

We talk about marriage and maybe having a kid of our own soon but I’m at the point of saying I don’t see that kind of future with us because of this. It’s lifestyle incompatibility I didn’t realize would matter so much…

Am I overreacting on a small issue/incompatibility? I honestly can’t tell. This is my first healthy relationship since my divorce 8 years ago so I’m trying to get it right and be that supportive gf/wife but I’m surprised how much I’m struggling to be happy like this. How much time apart is considered healthy and normal ?

I realize I feel more alone in this relationship than I’m comfortable with, and I rather be alone then in a relationship I feel alone in…but is this really worth ending things with someone you love? He’s obviously not going to change this part of his life nor would I want to make him because how meaningful would that be anyway.

text:

Until 6am. He did update me along the way this time but I just feel major "ick" about a grown man staying out all night long when we had plans today he'll likely be too tired to enjoy. I think it's actually less about the lack of communication and more that I feel really turned off by a 38yo man staying out all night when he promised he'd go home. Thanks for all the feedback. I'm feeling heartbroken and lost today.

I want to begin by saying that my partner is a wonderful person. There's so much about him; we get along really well, have so much in common, and we have so much fun together. However there's just one thing between us that seems to be causing a stumbling block in the relationship. He likes to go on nights out several times a week (although it has improved since we first started dating eight months ago) and when he goes out with friends - some of whom I know some of whom I don't - he often falls off the map of communication for many hours at a time, failing to let me know where he is or that he'll be out very late. We don't live together yet so I understand he isn't obligated to update me constantly, but it's just entire radio silence for long periods of time.

Now, I don't expect constant texting nor do I want that but I would like to know that he is okay, especially because there have been times he's been out and had some problems (such as running into an ex and having an argument, losing his phone, and one time even losing his shoes!) He never did find the phone or shoes, and I had to get him a new phone and help him set it up. He is not very technologically literate which I don't mind and I genuinely don't mind that he doesn't want to sit there on his phone texting me, that is absolutely fine, but I would really like for him to check in with me and let me know when his plans change or he decides to be out so late.

Sometimes he even says he'll call me or we'll FaceTime at a certain time and he entirely misses that window of time by hours because I give up and go to sleep. I never send him harassing messaging asking where he is or call repeatedly, I just stop trying at all to communicate at this point when I find out he's on a new "adventure".

We have gotten to the point where we even have Life360 on our phones so I can see where he is when I don't know where he is for example, at 2am or later. Sometimes he even goes out until this time of night or later when he has work the next morning he works in a professional career with children and is my belief that it is irresponsible to go out so late the night before you have work.

He seems to know that this behaviour is poor. We have had many discussions about it and he always says that he is sorry and that he feels ashamed, and he points out that it has improved since we met. However I still feel distraught every time it does happen. I don't think that he's an alcoholic although he does drink to excess when he has these disappearing spells, and he doesn't seem to have much control over what he does once he starts drinking and getting lead astray as he puts it by his friends. He has friends he'll "bump into" and they sort of make him distracted and take him into their evening (all nighter) plans.

By the way, he's a 38-year-old man. He has never been married and does not have children. I am a 34-year-old woman. I have two children from a prior relationship. My new partner says emphatically that someday he would like to be married and have a child together. However this one piece of his behavioural make-up is causing me to doubt whether or not he would be able to responsibly handle these things in the future. Everything else about him is absolutely ideal. He is kind, generous, smart, attentive. I love him so much and I want to make it work.

Am I just overreacting to these periods of disappearing? Am I asking too much for him to shoot me a quick text when his night plans change or go on till the wee hours of the morning? Is it something I should just learn to live with? I am totally open to feedback on this. Thank you.

text:
AIO for hating my extended family
I (14M) live in a Hispanic household, with lots of family near and far in places. I can’t speak much Spanish but I understand it, which makes communication not that difficult and easy to connect with relatives. But last year i went on a road trip to Oklahoma for a wedding with lots of family, some driving and most flying, I got stuck driving with some of my family. When we got there, all the family gathered/ grouped up at a house that the groom (another family member) was letting us stay in. It was uncomfortable and awkward being there since family I didn’t know or didn’t come around often were there, but I couldn’t do anything about it so I had to power through it. After the wedding tho not much happened besides conversations between cousins, aunts, and grandparents. But every now and then I would catch bits and pieces of their conversations that made it sound like they were talking about me. I wanted to confront them because in the moment it was confusing and it made me insecure but let it go thinking I misheard things since I have a past of misunderstanding certain topics especially in other languages. But on the way back from the trip I could never sleep in the car so I ended up staying awake with the driver which was my grandfather and my aunts. And almost every night they would talk in hushed tones to make sure not to wake nor let anybody hear. But on one particular night I heard my aunts having a conversation about the family in general, they would say things about their siblings, grandchildren, nieces, nephews, and their children. But when they reached my name I heard nothing but insults, I was baffled just hearing them talk shit about me at night thinking I was asleep with things like, “his voice is very annoying, such a fucking pig eating a lot, and I don’t know how he expects to get a girlfriend with looks like his.” For context I am not in the best shape being sort of chubby but not fat, my voice is high because I am gay and sometimes I can’t control how I say things with sass or flamboyance, and I am not the best looking and I know that but the way they talked about me made me angry and very insecure. After that trip I stayed away from Almost all extended family since when we came back I heard the same conversation come around when I came over for a cousins Bday. I would always get harsh and mocking glares and felt like eyes were always on me. But when I came to my mother and some other trusting family about the situation they said, “that wasn’t probably what I heard and they would never do that” when that was exactly what I heard them say and do. And after back and fourth banter about, “they are your family they love you” i ended up spiraling a that night and many nights after and eventually convinced myself that I was being an asshole and overreacting about the situation and started to take therapy. My therapist says that conversations in other languages can get confusing and mixed up and that may not what I might have heard. But I am still having second thoughts and on the fence about it. So Reddit AIO ? (Sorry for the long story T^T)

text:
AIO being overlooked for a senior position
I've been working i a company for almost 4 years. I am the longest serving member of staff. The boss of my department left in December. I applied for his job, but I didn't expect to get it because I wasn't qualified enough, but they'd done underqualified internal hires before so I thought why not apply? Fast forward a couple of months. despite frequently seeing the boss above my old boss (let's call her the main boss), she never acknowledged my application. Bear in mind I've never had any discipline, attendance or competence issues in my time here. In that time about 6 new staff started, and I had been asked to train them up and generally do a lot of the managerial duties that had been done by the previous manager.

I continued with some of these tasks as and when asked, and there was literally nobody doing any senior work apart from me. Then one day we all get an email asking who would like a smaller regular workload in return for some managerial duties? Me and this one new guy who I had trained up, let's call him Derek, reply yes. I figure maybe this will be like an audition for me. Then I get given a few basic tasks I'd been doing already since December, but she gives almost all other managerial work to him. Fast forward 6 more months, and without announcing, advertising, soliciting applications, nor interviewing (nor telling me about the existence of this new position) she calls me into her office and tells me she's decided to give Derek a senior, supervisory role (and I guess about a 20% pay bump) while we wait for a more qualified manager applicant. He is less experienced than me, has the same level of qualifications, is more or less the joint youngest guy in office (of about 20 people) and he has been here about 7 or 8 months, and I even trained him up.

It's not obvious there are other promotion opportunities available that won't require me to be given the position by the same boss, who has overlooked me in this situation. Unfortunately, there aren't many or any other jobs nearby with as good a salary as I am already on, but am I overreacting in feeling really insulted and offended and wanting to quit as soon as possible? A further question, assuming Derek is generally a decent guy and just looking out for his own situation, would you feel any animosity to him or just the main boss?

text:
AIO: Previous job ghosting me after asking about back pay
Back story- I worked for a company that was family owned and while I was there they refused to pay us overtime pay. After a few employees reported them, I guess they were ordered to issue back pay checks.

I moved to Oregon last year, quit working for them in December 2023, so I was surprised when a manager from one of the locations reached out for address and said a check would be mailed to me by end of March.

Fast forward to now, manager no longer works there so I contacted our accounting person in mid-May. She said she’d “look into it next week when in office”. It is now mid-June, she stopped responding to me, I contacted one of the owners June 1st and he had the same response and now has read my message asking for follow up, but not responded.

I sent the accounting/payroll person this today-

I went to the post office today to figure out my mailing issue. The check should be deliverable now. text:
AIO for defending my husband and telling my mom not to compare him to my father, EVER and getting angry about it? I 26F feel my mother 44F is utterly blinded by my father’s 48M behavior, and I believe there’s absolutely *no* comparison between my husband Liam 27M, myself, and my father. Recent events have dredged up old traumas and difficult conversations between my mom and me, yet her response has been to deflect and shift the blame onto us. She often claims her “brain is broken” and that she doesn’t know what to think. Honestly, I believe she should have left my father years ago, when I was still a child. For context, they’ve been together since my mom was 16 and my dad 21. She had me just before turning 18.

To give you some background: I’ve always lived with my parents, who also lived with my maternal grandparents at first. My maw passed away in 2017, leaving just my papaw. In 2019, when I got together with Liam, he moved in soon after. We married in 2022 and now have three kids. In 2023, my brother Kai (21M, actually my cousin on my mom’s side) came to live with us, escaping an even more abusive household. In 2025, we finally moved into a separate house on the same property (about 500 feet from where my papaw still lives).

The latest conflict started when my mom asked for help to find out if my dad would cheat while sober. This led to an argument. I told her bluntly, “After all he’s done, that should be enough for you.” She replied, “Yes, but you can’t change how you feel about someone. Liam’s not much different, so I don’t want to hear that. People make mistakes.”

I was already angry, so I said, “Liam has never been like my father. Do not compare him to my dad. If he was, I’d have left him long ago.” My mom came back with, “Liam has done some stuff worse than your dad.” I clarified, “Liam and I have made mistakes in our marriage that we’ve worked through. But our kids have never witnessed or been victims to any physical or emotional abuse from either of us. I suffered abuse from my father and still face trauma, and our kids have witnessed his emotional abuse twice. If Liam ever did even half of what my dad did, I’d have left him.”

Mom accused us of acting as if we’re “angels” and them “monsters,” pointing out that before we moved the kids had been living in a mess and that was on us, saying it made us just as bad. I disagreed, and the conversation left me feeling furious and misunderstood.

You all can be the judge, I’m being entirely honest about our circumstances.

I tried to move out in 2018, but my mother manipulated me into staying. Liam and I have made some bad choices. In 2020, when I was pregnant with our daughter (born December 2020), we planned to move again, but again my mom manipulated me into staying. We had two more kids (a son in 2022, another son in 2023), so our whole family was crammed into one bedroom (as this was a three bedroom home). We know that was a bad decision. But with constant drama, threats (my mom even threatened CPS when we tried to leave in 2023), and our own marital struggles, our mental health suffered. Our room became messy, honestly, it was disgusting and neither of us had the energy to maintain it.

In hindsight, CPS probably should’ve gotten involved. I don’t think our kids would’ve been taken, but maybe we’d have gotten some outside help and support we needed. Although our children’s needs were met and they were happy and healthy, no one should have to live in that kind of environment. There’s no excuse. Even though we loved our children deeply, I would’ve considered us bad parents then.

Despite these struggles, Liam and I faced our mistakes and worked through them. Our kids have never witnessed or experienced any abuse from us. Liam has never raised his hand at me or the kids, nor yelled at or threatened us. He’s gentle and supportive, always there when I break down.

After we moved into a new home in 2025, things improved dramatically. We now pay all the bills, essentials, and provide for our children completely on our own. I would consider us good parents now and doing the best we can. Our kids are our top priority. Everyone has their own space, and the environment is much better, though still affected by overarching family toxicity. (Technically this is still my parents home, although when building it they told us it was primarily for us, referring to me, Liam, and the kids).

Liam’s biggest struggles stemmed from our marital issues. Once we worked through them, he improved. I sought therapy a month or two after moving out and began to understand the depth of my trauma. I’ve been diagnosed with ADHD, CPTSD, anxiety, and bipolar disorder (and suspected autism). Through therapy and reflection, I realized my mom has been emotionally abusive (though perhaps not intentionally), and my father has been both physically and emotionally abusive, always apologizing, but never truly changing or fully being accountable.

The only life I’ve known is this toxic family environment. I stayed for so long because I didn’t realize how bad it was, and also because my mom manipulated and threatened me any time I tried to leave. When I recently told her we would be leaving definitively, she broke down, got loud, and threw tantrums. But we’re now waiting on a loan and will be out as soon as possible. My children’s wellbeing is my main motivation.

Financially, we’ve been able to move before when we wanted to and I was manipulated into staying. Now (even though we still live with my parents and it’s their home), we pay for everything. My husband works, I stay home with the kids and manage the house (except for dishes, Liam does those).

To illustrate why my father should never be compared to my husband, here are just a *few* examples of his behavior:

-When I was about five, after dinner, I asked for chips. My dad refused and my mom advocated for me. They argued and I remember him pinning her against the wall by the throat. That’s a terrifying thing for a child to see.

-My parents argued frequently when I was young, usually ending with my mom crying and my dad disappearing for hours, never helping to comfort or support her. This trauma has left me with a constant fear that my own husband might suddenly leave me, even though he’s never shown any signs of this.

-Although my father never actually hit anyone (except for typical “whoopings” and beating me with a belt as a child, which still isn’t right and is technically hitting), he’s been emotionally and physically abusive. If I misbehaved, he’d grab and shake me, grip my face and force eye contact, something I still can’t tolerate and may be linked to my ADHD or suspected autism. Would get nose to nose when he gripped my face. When I self harmed as a teen, he tried to “discipline” me by grabbing and scolding me, utterly lacking empathy (forcing eye contact then too). Even today, someone being in my face will trigger me, except with Liam and my kids.

-The latest, and most horrifying, incident happened on May 28th. My dad and Kai went swimming in the pond; my dad was drunk, Kai had been drinking. Not long after, my five year old daughter came to get me, saying “Hiyou (kids nickname) need you.” Kai, visibly shaken, told me my father had stripped, hugged him, kissed him on the cheek and said I love you, tried kissing on the lips (which Kai blocked), tried to force him into sexual contact, and confessed attraction to him. My dad tried to deny it, but I have no doubt about what happened and it’s beyond traumatizing for all involved.

So, am I overreacting in this case? I don’t think so, but would like some outside perspective.

text:
AIO for dreading Christmas because my SIL insists on hosting?
Last year was the first my SIL decided she wanted EVERYONE over at her tiny apartment on Christmas Day. By everyone I mean their family of four, my other SIL, MIL and her husband, my husband and I, BIL’s mom, her other two preteen kids (BIL’s siblings), and GIL. The living room which is the only space for everyone is maybe 10x10’, and it was already a tight squeeze without my baby, who will be going this year. There’s no flow to their home and zero seating. Everyone was just hovering waiting for a chair and squeezing past each other to get there. BIL’s mom brought huge stacks of presents for SIL, BIL, the nephews, and BIL’s two preteen siblings. It felt like she did it to one-up MIL, who has made it clear she only does one present per adult couple and the rest for the grandkids. Idk it just put a bad taste in my mouth as everyone else had to sit through an hour of them opening individually. wrapped. shein shirts and Temu toys 🫩

This year, since we had a baby, I assumed Christmas would be at MIL’s since she has a house with space for all of us that we go to for literally every other holiday or event. We were on the phone with MIL the other day and she told us about everyone going to SILs again. SIL has never officially invited us or told us what time to be there. She was talking to MIL as we were sitting at MIL’s house for her birthday yesterday and suddenly says she wants everyone at her place at 11am Christmas morning. Before I could say anything MIL basically told her “hell no” and she wanted to sleep in (lmfao love her). SIL then starts listing off a bunch of food she’s making and says, “I just have sooo much to do, it’s stressing me out.”

The food situation is another headache in itself. Last year it was room temperature finger foods and dips cramped on a tiny table everyone had to squeeze past to even get into the living room. The thing that annoys me most is she clearly doesn’t have the ability, capacity, or space to execute the food a proper way for hosting, yet refuses to ask for help other than demanding specific dishes, but not providing the link or recipe for them.

For reference: I am a cook by trade. I made 90% of the Thanksgiving for both of our families. They also know I’m already making cookie boxes for Christmas presents!!! So MIL asks her, “oh do you want me to bring anything?” and SIL says, “no. but I do want this,” showing MIL a TikTok of a recipe she wants. MIL turns to me and goes, “I think SouthernDefinition wanted to make something, that looks like a cake walk for her.” I ended up being shown the video. It does look super easy, but not appetizing. I am now stuck buying stuff for and making this thing I’m not gonna eat. A spinach puff pastry snowflake doesn’t scream delicious to me, personally. SIL then goes, “oh, also you should add salt to the butter to make it more savory” 🧍🏼‍♀️ if I wanted savory, SALT would not be my go-to method nor would I take culinary advice from you but, okeeeey… She still hasn’t sent me the video or a recipe nor has she officially invited us and told us when to be there.

What’s making this harder is my husband still wants to go despite all of this and despite me bitching about it since it happened. If this were at my MIL’s house, who actually has space and seating and a functional layout, I’d be way more inclined to go. It’s not that I don’t want to go at all. I just want a stress free first Christmas with my baby, and this feels like walking straight into a situation I already know is going to be uncomfortable, overstimulating, and mentally exhausting.

I love my in-laws and genuinely think I got lucky overall, but this is just wearing on my nerves. I’m contemplating just making whatever I want instead of the stupid snowflake and just saying I never got a recipe. Or, I’ll just send my husband alone and empty handed 🤷‍♀️

text:
AIO? Autistic kid bullying my daughter
So basically my daughter has been complaining about a boy that is constantly bugging her and her friends. He’s in the same grade as her but they are in different classes. They usually see each other during recess or the afterschool program. It’s gotten to the point where she’s come home telling me he’s hit her, pushed her, or called her rude things.

When questioned if she’s said anything to the kid, teachers, or staff she said she has but they don’t do anything about it because he’s “special”. I asked her to explain a bit further. Apparently the kid has some form of autism (not sure the extent) and they excuse it because of it. She’ll tell the boy “can you please move” and he’ll say “no” & get in front of them & bother them by either pushing them or mocking them.

I received a call from the vice principal yesterday regarding an incident with my daughter and a boy. My daughter was hit on the head with a tetherball and was crying at the nurses office. I asked if it happened to be the same boy who’s been bugging her and he said he would ask. It seemed to be accidental and a different kid but my daughter mentioned the other boy bugging her. I asked if he could figure out what was going on because I didn’t want my daughter getting bullied. He said he would stay afterschool to speak with the person in charge.

Close to the time for pickup I received a call from the afterschool person. She was pretty rude and condescending with me and I had to tell her to not talk to me with attitude I was just trying to see what is going on with my daughter. I don’t want her getting bullied and I didn’t understand where her hostility was coming from. She acted like she didn’t know what was going on and wasn’t aware of my child’s situation. She said my daughter didn’t tell her anything and she knows because she writes everything down.

She then proceeded to try and tell me my daughter hit someone a few months ago and how she “bugs other kids too”, which I was never made aware of. I asked why hadn’t she told me & she said she didn’t feel the need to. It was a whole back and forth after that but we stayed professional the rest of the convo. She then offered to have a meeting with me and my child but it would not involve the school and she said neither the principal nor the vice principal would be there because afterschool program is a different department. Idk if it’s true or not but I didn’t care. I agreed and she said she’d call me back MAYBE in a week, and if she didn’t call me in a week for me to call her.

Well today she called me again and said she knew about the situation and she knew who the kid was but that he’s special. Her tone was different. She spoke respectful and without attitude. I asked what she meant by that and she said “well he has an IEP” and asked if I knew what that was. I don’t. Then she said that he “has a bit of autism and he doesn’t know how to act”. I understand there’s a whole spectrum for autism and every case is different but when did it become okay to dismiss wrong behavior?

I let her know I understood that but it still didn’t mean it was okay to put hands on my child or to be constantly bugging her and for them to be dismissive of it. She said she can talk to the boy’s mom and we can go from there. AIO for thinking they aren’t taking my daughter serious because of the kid’s autism? AIO for assuming they dismiss his behavior because of his diagnosis?
